title: Epic — Mathematical Foundations & Leapfrog Theory (the alien-artifact program)
description:
# Epic — Mathematical Foundations & Leapfrog Theory

## The thesis (read this first)
Strip away the biological metaphors and the bio-inspired synaptic transformer is ONE precise mathematical object: a **stochastic, gauge-covariant, metriplectic neural stochastic differential equation on a fiber bundle, with cleanly separated timescales**. The base manifold is the token/sequence; the FIBER over each token is the synaptic state (calcium, vesicle pools, energy, fast/slow weights, expert genome); the CONNECTION transports memory across timescales; the bracket is reversible (Hamiltonian) + irreversible (dissipative); the noise is the stochastic vesicle release. Almost every deep, esoteric branch of modern mathematics maps onto exactly one stratum of this object — and using the RIGHT branch per stratum yields provable capabilities that conventional transformers structurally cannot have.

## Why this is the leapfrog (not incremental)
Conventional transformers are static affine maps + softmax with no state, no conserved quantities, no certified stability, no hierarchical memory geometry, no compositional guarantees. Because THIS substrate has genuine multi-timescale dynamics, we can import the heavy machinery that physics/geometry/probability developed for exactly such systems and obtain things a vanilla transformer cannot: unconditional stability by construction, thermodynamically-calibrated uncertainty, certified working-memory retention, hierarchical exponential-capacity recall, topologically-principled architecture growth, and gauge-geometric continual learning with forgetting bounds.

## Discipline (alien-artifact contract — applies to every thrust)
NO ornamental math. Each thrust must: (1) compile its math into RUNTIME ARTIFACTS (certificates, monitors, structure-preserving integrators, guard tables); (2) state a falsifiable LEAPFROG HYPOTHESIS with a BASELINE COMPARATOR; (3) carry PROOF OBLIGATIONS (assumptions / statement / verification / failure conditions); (4) ship a DETERMINISTIC CONSERVATIVE FALLBACK to the plain engineering baseline (the vg9/yw9 code) so a failed bet never breaks training; (5) pass the EV/relevance/risk gate recorded in the thrust.

## The 8 thrusts, ranked by expected value (EV = Impact x Confidence / Effort)
- A. Metriplectic / GENERIC synaptic dynamics (stability + conservation BY CONSTRUCTION). KEYSTONE.
- F. Singular-perturbation + catastrophe-theoretic working memory (certified retention; also the timescale-separation backbone that LICENSES composing all other thrusts). KEYSTONE.
- E. Stochastic-thermodynamic uncertainty (TUR/fluctuation-theorem-CALIBRATED UQ).
- B. Ultrametric / replica-symmetry-breaking hierarchical memory (hierarchical exponential capacity).
- C. Free-probabilistic + topological structural plasticity (spectrally-safe, topology-triggered neurogenesis).
- H. Tropical / idempotent geometric skeleton (exact interpretability + certified robustness).
- D. Gauge-theoretic multi-timescale consolidation (forgetting = curvature/holonomy; geodesic schedules).
- G. Operadic / sheaf-theoretic compositional binding (provable systematic generalization).

## Composition is SAFE because the timescales are separated
calcium (fast) << vesicle/release (medium) << fast-weights (slow) << slow-weights/consolidation (slower) << structure (slowest). This is a genuine singular-perturbation hierarchy (Thrust F / Fenichel theory), which is precisely the timescale-separation statement the composition-compatibility discipline requires: the families act on non-interfering strata.

## Acceptance criteria
- Each thrust has a written theory note with proof obligations, compiled artifacts, a falsifiable hypothesis tested against a baseline with stats (74f), and a verified deterministic fallback. The unified-foundation capstone ties A/D/E/F into one geometric object.

Relates: yw9 (yw9), sax (sax), uta (uta), cel (cel), u2t (u2t), the MGR cross-pollination epic (vap), and eqyk/74f for verification.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — theory thrust (alien-artifact discipline; in ADDITION to the standard DoD: unit tests + e2e in eqyk + detailed logging + default-off toggle + docs):
1. THEORY NOTE committed under docs/theory/ with the derivation + an explicit PROOF-OBLIGATION table: assumptions / formal statement / verification method / failure conditions / rollback.
2. Math COMPILED TO RUNTIME ARTIFACTS (certificate / monitor / structure-preserving integrator / guard table). Naming math without a runtime artifact is NOT done.
3. FALSIFIABLE leapfrog hypothesis tested against the NAMED baseline comparator with statistics (74f.3). A null/negative result is a valid, publishable outcome — report it honestly; do not p-hack.
4. DETERMINISTIC CONSERVATIVE FALLBACK to the engineering baseline, with a verified trigger, so a failed mathematical bet never breaks training (budgeted mode).
5. Composition with other thrusts GATED by the timescale-separation certificate (0642.10); record the timescale-separation statement.
NOT done until artifacts verify, the proof obligation holds (or is honestly reported failed), and the fallback is tested.

title: Thrust A — Metriplectic/GENERIC Synaptic Dynamics (stability & conservation by construction)
description:
# Thrust A — Metriplectic / GENERIC Synaptic Dynamics  [KEYSTONE]

## Math families (EV-gated, natural fit)
GENERIC (General Equation for Non-Equilibrium Reversible-Irreversible Coupling) + port-Hamiltonian systems + contact geometry + Lyapunov/Barrier-SOS. Composition: 2 families (geometric mechanics + Lyapunov), synergistic (++), timescale-local.

## The mathematics
Write the full synaptic state z = (Ca, vesicle pools, energy, W_fast, W_slow, ...). GENERIC posits
  dz/dt = L(z) dE/dz + M(z) dS/dz
with L skew-symmetric (reversible/Poisson, conserves energy E) and M symmetric PSD (irreversible/dissipative, produces entropy S), subject to the DEGENERACY CONDITIONS L dS/dz = 0 and M dE/dz = 0. These conditions algebraically GUARANTEE dE/dt = 0 and dS/dt = (dS/dz)·M·(dS/dz) >= 0. The free energy F = E - TS is then a Lyapunov function.

## Bio mapping (this is not a metaphor — it IS the structure)
Vesicle conservation (RRP+RES+in-flight = const) is a Casimir of L; metabolic energy dissipation is the M-part producing entropy; calcium influx/decay is reversible+dissipative. The forward pass becomes a STRUCTURE-PRESERVING INTEGRATOR (discrete gradient / symplectic) of this flow.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
A GENERIC-structured synaptic transformer is UNCONDITIONALLY STABLE by construction (the free energy decreases monotonically; trajectories are bounded WITHOUT any clamps), trains at higher learning rates without divergence, and — because the reversible L-part is exactly invertible — supports O(1)-memory reversible backprop natively (subsuming the MGR reversible-blocks idea). Baseline comparator: the clamp-and-pray Euler step in vg9 (vg9.5/vg9.7). Predicted: >= same loss with ZERO NaN/divergence events across an LR sweep where the baseline diverges.

## Artifacts
Free-energy Lyapunov MONITOR (must be non-increasing); a discrete-gradient / GENERIC INTEGRATOR (the forward step) with machine-precision energy-conservation + entropy-nonneg GUARDS; a degeneracy-condition guard table; an entropy-production ledger.

## Proof obligation (#15 Stability + #1 from library)
Assumptions: brackets satisfy Jacobi + degeneracy (structural). Statement: dE/dt=0, dS/dt>=0 => F is Lyapunov => bounded trajectories. Verify: symbolic/grid check of degeneracy; runtime energy/entropy monitors in e2e. Failure: degeneracy violated by a learned L/M. Fallback: revert that step to the clamped Euler baseline; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~5.0 (Impact 5 x Conf 4 / Effort 4). Relevance: the biophysics LITERALLY is a thermodynamic system. Risk: learned L/M break degeneracy -> countermeasure: parameterize L via skew matrices and M via PSD factors so degeneracy holds by construction; fallback to baseline.

3. FALSIFIABLE leapfrog hypothesis tested against the NAMED baseline comparator with statistics (74f.3). A null/negative result is a valid, publishable outcome — report it honestly; do not p-hack.
4. DETERMINISTIC CONSERVATIVE FALLBACK to the engineering baseline, with a verified trigger, so a failed mathematical bet never breaks training (budgeted mode).
5. Composition with other thrusts GATED by the timescale-separation certificate (0642.10); record the timescale-separation statement.
NOT done until artifacts verify, the proof obligation holds (or is honestly reported failed), and the fallback is tested.

title: Theory note, proof obligations & assumptions ledger — Metriplectic/GENERIC
description: Derive the GENERIC form for the full synaptic state z: identify the energy E, entropy S, skew Poisson L, PSD friction M, and the Casimirs (vesicle conservation). PROVE the degeneracy conditions L·dS/dz=0, M·dE/dz=0 hold for the chosen parameterization, hence dE/dt=0 and dS/dt>=0, hence F=E-TS is Lyapunov => bounded trajectories. Write the proof-obligation table (assumptions: Jacobi+degeneracy structural; verification: symbolic+grid check; failure: learned L/M break degeneracy; fallback: clamped Euler). Deliverable: docs/theory/metriplectic.md. Baseline comparator named: vg9 clamped step (vg9.5/vg9.7).

title: Subtask: identify energy E(z) and entropy S(z) functionals for the synaptic state
description:
## Sub-problem
Define the ENERGY functional E(z) and ENTROPY functional S(z) for the synaptic state z=(Ca, BUF, RRP, RES, PR, energy, W_fast, W_slow,...). These are the two potentials the GENERIC flow dz=L·∇E+M·∇S descends/conserves.

## Concrete proposal (refine in the note)
- E(z): the STORED/reversible energy — vesicle "potential" (pool occupancy), calcium-buffer elastic energy, and a weight elastic term ½||W||². 
- S(z): the irreversible/heat-like potential — a concave occupancy entropy of the vesicle pools and a calcium-dissipation term, chosen so its gradient drives the depletion/decay we already model.
Calibrate so that dz=L∇E+M∇S reproduces the current `release()` update to first order (behavior preservation).

## Why it serves the thrust
E and S are the objects whose conservation/monotonicity give unconditional stability. Getting them right (with units) is prerequisite for L, M, the degeneracy conditions, and the Lyapunov certificate.

## Acceptance
Committed E(z), S(z) with units and a first-order reduction to the current dynamics; sign/convexity checked.

title: Subtask: construct the skew Poisson operator L and verify the Jacobi identity
description:
## Sub-problem
Build the skew-symmetric operator L(z) (L=-L^T) encoding the REVERSIBLE couplings of the synapse (calcium<->vesicle priming, complexin gating, the symplectic weight pairing). For a genuine Poisson bracket {F,G}=∇F·L·∇G the Jacobi identity must hold; otherwise use a Lie-Poisson / Dirac structure that satisfies it BY CONSTRUCTION.

## Approach
Prefer a constant or Lie-Poisson L (Jacobi automatic) or a Dirac structure; if state-dependent, verify Jacobi symbolically on the reduced state and numerically on a grid.

## Why
L is the reversible engine; without Jacobi the "energy-conserving" claim is false. Skewness gives dE/dt|_L = ∇E·L·∇E = 0 identically.

## Acceptance
L skew by construction; Jacobi verified (symbolic or grid); documented which couplings are reversible vs dissipative.

title: Subtask: construct the PSD friction operator M = B^T B (dissipation)
description:
## Sub-problem
Build the symmetric positive-semidefinite friction operator M(z) encoding IRREVERSIBLE processes: calcium decay, metabolic energy use, vesicle relaxation, fast-weight forgetting. Parameterize M=B(z)^T B(z) so PSD holds BY CONSTRUCTION (no runtime check can fail).

## Why
M·∇S drives dissipation; PSD guarantees dS/dt=∇S·M·∇S≥0 (entropy production non-negative) — half of the stability guarantee. B^T B parameterization makes this unconditional and keeps M learnable.

## Acceptance
M=B^T B implemented as a learnable factor; mapping of each dissipative bio-process to a block of M documented.

title: Subtask: enforce & prove the degeneracy conditions L·∇S=0, M·∇E=0
description:
## Sub-problem
The GENERIC consistency conditions L·∇S=0 (entropy is a Casimir of the reversible part) and M·∇E=0 (energy unaffected by dissipation) are what make E conserved and S monotone simultaneously. Enforce them BY CONSTRUCTION: e.g. project M onto the orthogonal complement of ∇E, and choose S in the kernel of L (or build L on the leaves of constant S).

## Proof
With degeneracy: dE/dt = ∇E·L·∇E + ∇E·M·∇S = 0 + 0 = 0; dS/dt = ∇S·L·∇E + ∇S·M·∇S = 0 + (≥0) ≥ 0.

## Acceptance
Construction guaranteeing both conditions; the two-line proof committed; a runtime degeneracy guard table (tolerance) specified.

Depends on the E/S/L/M constructions.

title: Subtask: Lyapunov theorem (free energy F=E-TS) + domain-of-attraction
description:
## Sub-problem
State and prove: under the degeneracy conditions, F=E−T·S (T a fixed reference temperature) is non-increasing (dF/dt=−T·dS/dt≤0), hence sublevel sets {F≤c} are FORWARD-INVARIANT and trajectories are bounded — the unconditional-stability guarantee. Give a domain-of-attraction estimate (largest sublevel set inside the valid state region).

## Why
This is the headline guarantee ("cannot blow up by construction"). It is the proof-obligation #15 (Stability) the artifacts must monitor.

## Acceptance
Committed theorem + proof + domain-of-attraction estimate; the full proof-obligation table (assumptions/statement/verification/failure/fallback).

Depends on the degeneracy subtask.

title: Subtask: reversible L-flow => native O(1)-memory reversible backprop
description:
## Sub-problem
The reversible (L) part of the flow is exactly invertible. Derive the closed-form inverse step so activations can be RECOMPUTED in the backward pass, giving O(1) activation memory — subsuming the MGR reversible-blocks idea (vap.3) but as a CONSEQUENCE of the geometry, not a bolt-on. Specify how the dissipative (M) part is handled (checkpoint the small dissipative correction).

## Why
Memory is the first-class bottleneck for stateful synapses; this unlocks longer context / bigger models for free, and ties Thrust A to vap.3.

## Acceptance
Inverse-step derivation; memory-budget analysis; correctness plan (gradcheck vs standard autograd).

title: Compile to runtime artifacts + implementation (toggle + deterministic fallback) — Metriplectic/GENERIC
description: Implement the structure-preserving (discrete-gradient/symplectic) integrator as the forward step, with L parameterized as skew (A-A^T) and M as PSD (B^T B) so degeneracy holds BY CONSTRUCTION. Compile artifacts: free-energy Lyapunov MONITOR (assert non-increasing), energy-conservation + entropy-nonneg GUARDS (machine precision), degeneracy guard table, entropy-production ledger. Behind a default-off toggle; deterministic FALLBACK to the clamped Euler baseline when a guard trips. Bounded overhead.

title: Subtask: implement the discrete-gradient (structure-preserving) integrator
description:
## Sub-problem
Implement the synaptic forward step as a DISCRETE-GRADIENT / average-vector-field integrator z_{t+1}=z_t+dt·[L·\bar∇E+M·\bar∇S] using a discrete gradient \bar∇ (Gonzalez) so that E is conserved EXACTLY and S is monotone at the DISCRETE level (not just continuous). This is the core runtime object.

## Why
A naive Euler step destroys the conservation the theory guarantees; the discrete gradient preserves it to machine precision, making the Lyapunov certificate hold for the actual code.

## Acceptance
Integrator implemented; discrete energy drift ≤ 1e-10 on a test; reduces to baseline behavior at first order; bounded overhead measured.

title: Subtask: free-energy Lyapunov MONITOR + detailed logging
description:
## Sub-problem
Implement the runtime MONITOR that logs F=E−TS, E, S, and entropy production per step (via the eqyk.2 structured-logging schema) and asserts F is non-increasing within tolerance. This is the auditable evidence for the stability obligation.

## Why
Per the alien-artifact contract, the guarantee must be a runtime CERTIFICATE/monitor, not just a paper claim. Detailed per-step logging is required (user mandate) so we can SEE stability holding.

## Acceptance
Monitor emits rich + JSONL traces of E/S/F/entropy-production; flags any F increase; cheap by default; documented.

title: Subtask: conservation/entropy/degeneracy GUARD tables + fallback trigger
description:
## Sub-problem
Implement guard tables: energy drift ≤ ε_E, entropy production ≥ −ε_S, degeneracy residuals ||L∇S||,||M∇E|| ≤ ε_D. On any breach, trip the DETERMINISTIC FALLBACK (revert this step to the clamped-Euler baseline vg9.5/vg9.7) and log the event.

## Why
Budgeted-mode + conservative fallback discipline: a learned L/M that violates degeneracy must never break training.

## Acceptance
Guard tables wired to the integrator; injected violations correctly trip the fallback; events logged.

title: Subtask: SynapticConfig toggle (default-off) + ablation registry entry
description:
## Sub-problem
Add a documented `metriplectic_integrator` SynapticConfig flag (DEFAULT OFF), registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7), so the GENERIC integrator is a clean, toggleable experiment vs the baseline Euler step.

## Acceptance
Toggle present + documented + default-off; ablation preset added; validated by the config validator (hm4.7).

title: Subtask: UNIT TESTS for the integrator, monitors, guards & fallback
description:
## Sub-problem
Comprehensive unit tests (in the eqyk framework): (a) energy conserved to machine precision over many steps; (b) entropy production ≥0; (c) F non-increasing under random inputs; (d) degeneracy residuals ~0; (e) fallback fires on an injected degeneracy violation; (f) first-order agreement with the baseline step. With detailed assertion logging.

## Why
Per the user mandate and DoD: nothing is done until tests prove it works. These tests are the safety net for the keystone thrust.

## Acceptance
Test module committed + green in CI; covers integrator, monitor, guards, fallback; deterministic/seeded.

Depends on the integrator + monitor + guard subtasks.

title: Subtask: implement reversible backprop via the L-inverse (+ gradcheck)
description:
## Sub-problem
Implement O(1)-memory reversible backprop using the closed-form L-inverse from the theory subtask: recompute activations in backward, checkpoint only the small dissipative correction. Validate with gradcheck vs standard autograd and measure the memory reduction.

## Acceptance
Reversible path matches autograd gradients (gradcheck); measured VRAM reduction enabling a longer-context run; behind the toggle.

Depends on: 0642.1.1 reversible subtask + the integrator.

title: Falsification experiment vs baseline + verification — Metriplectic/GENERIC
description: Falsify the leapfrog claim: LR sweep where the clamped baseline diverges — assert the GENERIC integrator stays bounded (zero NaN/divergence) at >= equal loss, with the free-energy monitor non-increasing throughout. Verify conservation/entropy guards to tolerance; verify the fallback fires correctly on an injected degeneracy violation. Stats via 74f (74f.3).

title: Subtask: falsification harness — LR-sweep stability vs clamped-Euler baseline
description:
## Sub-problem
Build the e2e experiment: an LR/step-size sweep on a tiny bio model where the clamped-Euler baseline (vg9.5/vg9.7) DIVERGES; assert the GENERIC integrator stays bounded (zero NaN/divergence) at >= equal loss, with the free-energy monitor non-increasing throughout. Detailed per-run logging of the divergence boundary.

## Acceptance
Runnable e2e script; produces the stability-vs-LR curve for both arms; reproduces the predicted leapfrog (baseline diverges, GENERIC survives).

title: Subtask: statistical analysis vs baseline (paired, CIs) + result writeup
description:
## Sub-problem
Run multi-seed, route through the statistics layer (74f.3) for paired comparison + CIs on loss and divergence-rate; write the result (positive OR null — honest) to the results registry (hm4.1) and update docs/theory/metriplectic.md.

## Acceptance
Stats-backed comparison committed with CIs; honest verdict recorded; theory note updated.

Depends on: 0642.1.3 harness.

title: Subtask: runtime proof-obligation verification + fallback test
description:
## Sub-problem
Verify the proof obligation end-to-end on a real run: energy/entropy/degeneracy monitors stay within tolerance; then inject a degeneracy violation and confirm the deterministic fallback engages and training continues. This certifies both the guarantee AND its safety net.

## Acceptance
Obligation verified over a real run (logged evidence); fallback-trigger test passes.

Depends on: 0642.1.2 monitors/guards.

title: Timescale-separation certificate & composition-compatibility harness
description:
## Goal (composition KEYSTONE)
The alien-artifact discipline requires a timescale-separation statement before composing multiple math families. Provide the RUNTIME MEASUREMENT + GUARD that certifies the synapse's strata are actually separated: eps_k = (fast rate)/(slow rate) per coupling (calcium << release << fast-weights << slow-weights << structure). Below a threshold, the families are non-interfering and may be composed; above it, composition guards trip and thrusts fall back.

## Why
This is what makes stacking Thrusts A/E/F/etc. SAFE rather than ornamental (per the composition-compatibility matrix). Fenichel theory (Thrust F) is the math; this task is its operational guard.

## Artifacts
A per-layer timescale-ratio MONITOR (eps_k from measured rates / Jacobian eigenvalue gaps); a composition-eligibility guard table (which thrusts may co-activate given measured eps); an interference test harness (toggle pairs of thrusts, assert no cross-timescale coupling beyond tolerance).

## Acceptance
eps monitor live; composition-eligibility guard wired so certificates from each thrust are gated on measured separation; interference tests for every co-activated pair pass or auto-disable the higher-risk thrust.

Depends on: Thrust F theory (slow-manifold/eigenvalue-gap math).

title: Subtask: eps gauge measurement + per-coupling separation table
description:
## Sub-problem
Implement the runtime eps_k measurement (fast-Jacobian eigenvalue-gap, shared with 0642.2.1.1/0642.2.2.2) for every fast/slow coupling, producing a per-layer SEPARATION TABLE (calcium<<release<<fast-weights<<slow-weights<<structure).
## Acceptance
eps_k monitor + separation table exported; sanity values from a real model.

title: Subtask: composition-eligibility guard table (which thrusts may co-activate)
description:
## Sub-problem
Build the guard table mapping measured eps_k -> which thrust certificates remain valid when co-activated; below threshold = compose freely, above = trip the higher-risk thrust's fallback. This operationalizes the composition-compatibility discipline.
## Acceptance
Guard table wired so every thrust certificate is gated on measured separation; documented.

title: Subtask: pairwise interference test harness + UNIT TESTS + logging
description:
## Sub-problem
A harness that toggles PAIRS of thrusts and asserts no cross-timescale coupling beyond tolerance (the invariants of each still hold); auto-disable the higher-risk thrust if violated. Unit tests for representative pairs (A+F, A+E, F+B). Detailed logging.
## Acceptance
Interference harness + tests committed and green; co-activation safety demonstrated for key pairs.

title: Capstone: the synaptic transformer as a stochastic gauge-covariant metriplectic neural SDE on a fiber bundle
description:
## Goal (grand synthesis)
Write the master formal object from which Thrusts A (metriplectic bracket), D (gauge connection), E (noise/thermodynamics), F (timescale/slow-manifold) are special cases: a neural SDE
  dz = [ L(z)dE + M(z)dS ]dt + sigma(z) dW ,  on a fiber bundle (base = sequence, fiber = synaptic state),
with a connection A transporting the fiber across layers/timescales, GENERIC brackets (L,M) for the drift, and vesicle-noise diffusion sigma satisfying the fluctuation theorems. Learning is on the space of (brackets, connection, diffusion) subject to the structural conditions (degeneracy, gauge-covariance, normal hyperbolicity).

## Why
A single geometric object unifies the stability (A), consolidation (D), uncertainty (E), and memory (F) guarantees, and makes their COMPOSITION a theorem rather than a hope. This is the publishable theoretical core ("A Geometric Theory of Synaptic Transformers").

## Acceptance
A committed master theory note deriving each thrust as a restriction of the SDE; a consistency proof that the per-thrust guarantees compose under the timescale-separation certificate; identifies the minimal shared state/API the implementations must expose.

Depends on: Thrusts A, F (and informs E, D). Relates: yw9 (the differentiable-recurrence substrate this formalizes).

title: Subtask: derive each thrust (A,D,E,F) as a restriction of the master neural SDE
description:
## Sub-problem
Show A (metriplectic drift L dE+M dS), D (connection A / transport), E (diffusion sigma dW with FT structure), F (fast-slow / slow-manifold) are all RESTRICTIONS of the master object dz=[L dE+M dS]dt+sigma dW on a fiber bundle with connection A. Make the correspondences explicit.
## Acceptance
A committed table mapping each thrust to a component/limit of the SDE.

title: Subtask: composition-consistency proof (guarantees compose under timescale separation)
description:
## Sub-problem
Prove the per-thrust guarantees (Lyapunov stability, retention delta*, calibration, forgetting bound) COMPOSE without interfering, using the timescale-separation certificate (0642.10): each acts on a distinct stratum (fast/medium/slow), so their invariants are simultaneously preserved.
## Acceptance
A composition theorem + the interference-free argument; identifies any genuine tension pairs + reconciliation.

title: Subtask: define the minimal shared state/API the implementations must expose
description:
## Sub-problem
From the SDE, specify the minimal shared synaptic-state object + API (the fiber: z, the brackets L/M, the connection A, the diffusion sigma, the eps gauge) that every thrust implementation reads/writes, so the artifacts compose cleanly (ties to bio_telemetry hm4.6 + the differentiable substrate yw9).
## Acceptance
A committed shared-state/API spec adopted by the thrust artifact tasks.

title: Thrust F — Singular-Perturbation & Catastrophe-Theoretic Working Memory (certified retention)
description:
# Thrust F — Geometric Singular Perturbation + Catastrophe Theory  [KEYSTONE for composition]

## Math families
Geometric singular perturbation theory (Fenichel) + catastrophe theory (cusp) + fast-slow dynamical systems. 2 families, synergistic.

## The mathematics
The synapse is a singularly perturbed system: eps dx/dt = f(x,y) (fast: calcium, release), dy/dt = g(x,y) (slow: weights), eps<<1. FENICHEL's theorem: there is a normally-hyperbolic SLOW MANIFOLD M_eps = {x = h(y,eps)} that persists, attracts, and carries the reduced dynamics dy/dt = g(h(y),y). The CaMKII/PP1 latch is a CUSP CATASTROPHE: with state m and controls (a=calcium "splitting", b=BDNF "normal"), the equilibria solve m^3 + a m + b = 0; the fold curves 4a^3+27b^2=0 bound a HYSTERESIS region whose width is a closed-form function of (a,b).

## Bio mapping
Slow manifold = the MEMORY subspace (where W_slow lives); fast fibers = the transient COMPUTATION. The cusp gives the latch its bistability; the hysteresis half-width delta* is a COMPUTABLE noise-robustness margin.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
(1) CERTIFIED retention: a written memory survives any perturbation of magnitude < delta* (the hysteresis half-width) — a guarantee no softmax memory can make. (2) Optimal, minimal-energy WRITE/ERASE pulses derived from the cusp geometry. (3) A rigorous slow-manifold PROJECTOR that separates memory (slow) from computation (fast), giving a principled low-rank memory state. (4) THIS thrust supplies the timescale-separation statement that licenses composing all other thrusts. Baseline: the heuristic sigmoid latch in sax (sax.2). Predicted: longer retention-under-noise at equal capacity, with a certificate.

## Artifacts
Cusp write/erase PULSE TABLE; a RETENTION CERTIFICATE (delta*-robustness from hysteresis width); a slow-manifold PROJECTOR (reduced memory state + reconstruction error monitor); a normal-hyperbolicity monitor (eigenvalue-gap of the fast Jacobian).

## Proof obligation (#15)
Assumptions: normal hyperbolicity (fast-Jacobian eigenvalues bounded away from imaginary axis), eps small (timescale gap measured at runtime). Statement: slow manifold persists; cusp normal form => retention bound delta*. Verify: eigenvalue-gap monitor; bifurcation continuation of the latch; back-test retention under injected noise. Failure: timescale gap collapses (eps not small). Fallback: disable certified-mode, use heuristic latch; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~5.33 (Impact 4 x Conf 4 / Effort 3). Relevance: multi-timescale structure is intrinsic. Risk: eps not actually small at some layers -> measure eps via the eigenvalue gap; gate certificates on it.

1. THEORY NOTE committed under docs/theory/ with the derivation + an explicit PROOF-OBLIGATION table: assumptions / formal statement / verification method / failure conditions / rollback.
2. Math COMPILED TO RUNTIME ARTIFACTS (certificate / monitor / structure-preserving integrator / guard table). Naming math without a runtime artifact is NOT done.
3. FALSIFIABLE leapfrog hypothesis tested against the NAMED baseline comparator with statistics (74f.3). A null/negative result is a valid, publishable outcome — report it honestly; do not p-hack.
4. DETERMINISTIC CONSERVATIVE FALLBACK to the engineering baseline, with a verified trigger, so a failed mathematical bet never breaks training (budgeted mode).
5. Composition with other thrusts GATED by the timescale-separation certificate (0642.10); record the timescale-separation statement.

title: Theory note, proof obligations & assumptions ledger — Singular-perturbation/cusp
description: Formalize the fast-slow split (eps dx/dt=f, dy/dt=g); state Fenichel persistence of the normally-hyperbolic slow manifold M_eps={x=h(y,eps)} and the reduced flow. Derive the CaMKII/PP1 cusp normal form m^3+am+b=0 with fold set 4a^3+27b^2=0 and the closed-form hysteresis half-width delta*(a,b). Proof-obligation table: assumptions (normal hyperbolicity via fast-Jacobian eigenvalue gap; eps small, measured); statement (manifold persists; cusp => retention >= delta*); failure (eps not small); fallback (heuristic latch). Deliverable: docs/theory/singular_perturbation.md. Baseline: sax sigmoid latch (sax.2).

title: Subtask: formalize the fast-slow split & define the runtime eps gauge
description:
## Sub-problem
Partition the synaptic state into FAST (calcium, release probability) and SLOW (vesicle pools, weights) and write the singularly-perturbed system eps·dx/dt=f(x,y), dy/dt=g(x,y). Define the dimensionless separation gauges eps_k as ratios of the fast/slow rates, and specify their RUNTIME estimator (the spectral gap of the fast Jacobian ∂f/∂x). This gauge is reused by the composition keystone (0642.10).

## Why
Everything in Thrust F (and the license to compose all thrusts) rests on eps being small and measurable. Make it explicit and observable.

## Acceptance
Committed fast/slow partition + eps_k definitions + the eigenvalue-gap estimator; sanity values from a real model.

title: Subtask: Fenichel slow-manifold existence & the reduced (memory) flow
description:
## Sub-problem
State normal hyperbolicity (fast-Jacobian eigenvalues bounded away from the imaginary axis) and invoke Fenichel's theorem: a slow manifold M_eps={x=h(y,eps)} persists, attracts, and carries the reduced flow dy/dt=g(h(y),y). Compute h to first order in eps; identify the reduced flow as the EFFECTIVE MEMORY dynamics.

## Why
The slow manifold IS the memory subspace; the reduction rigorously separates transient computation (fast fibers) from durable memory (slow manifold).

## Acceptance
Committed normal-hyperbolicity condition + first-order h + reduced flow; numerical check that trajectories collapse onto M_eps.

title: Subtask: reduce the CaMKII/PP1 latch to the cusp normal form
description:
## Sub-problem
Show the CaMKII/PP1 consolidation latch is a CUSP catastrophe: via center-manifold + normal-form reduction bring it to m^3+a·m+b=0 with control map a=a(calcium) (splitting factor), b=b(BDNF) (normal factor). Derive the fold (bifurcation) set 4a^3+27b^2=0 bounding the bistable region.

## Why
The cusp geometry is what gives the latch certified bistability, retention, and optimal write/erase — replacing the heuristic sigmoid latch (sax.2) with a principled one.

## Acceptance
Normal-form reduction + the control map + the fold-set equation committed; matches the latch's measured bistability.

title: Subtask: closed-form hysteresis half-width delta* = the retention certificate
description:
## Sub-problem
From the cusp fold set derive the closed-form HYSTERESIS HALF-WIDTH delta*(a,b): the minimal perturbation that flips a latched bit. This is the CERTIFIED retention margin — a stored memory provably survives any noise of magnitude < delta*.

## Why
A guarantee no softmax memory can make. It is the runtime certificate the artifact must compute and the experiment must show is TIGHT.

## Acceptance
delta*(a,b) formula + proof that retention >= delta*; the proof-obligation table (assumptions: cusp validity, eps small; failure: eps large; fallback: heuristic latch).

title: Subtask: optimal minimal-energy write/erase pulses from cusp geometry
description:
## Sub-problem
Use the cusp geometry to derive the MINIMAL-ENERGY control trajectory (calcium/BDNF pulse) that crosses the fold to flip the latch ON or OFF — the optimal write/erase operation. Contrast with the current un-shaped gating.

## Acceptance
Pulse-shape table (write vs erase) with energy cost; demonstrated to flip the latch reliably with minimal drive.

title: Subtask: slow-manifold projector as the low-rank memory state
description:
## Sub-problem
Define the projector onto the slow manifold as a principled LOW-RANK memory representation (the durable state), with a reconstruction-error bound measuring how much "memory" is lost by the reduction. This gives a rigorous compressed memory and a monitor.

## Acceptance
Projector + reconstruction-error bound; the reduced memory state exposed via bio_telemetry (hm4.6).

Implement behind a toggle; FALLBACK to the heuristic latch when the eigenvalue gap (eps) is too large to certify.

title: Thrust E — Stochastic-Thermodynamic Uncertainty (fluctuation-theorem-calibrated UQ)
description:
# Thrust E — Stochastic Thermodynamics of Vesicle Release

## Math families
Stochastic thermodynamics (Jarzynski/Crooks fluctuation theorems) + Thermodynamic Uncertainty Relation (TUR) + large deviations. 2 families.

## The mathematics
Each stochastic vesicle-release event is a nonequilibrium process with entropy production Sigma. The TUR states Var(J)/<J>^2 >= 2/<Sigma> for any current J: PRECISION COSTS ENTROPY (energy). Crooks: P_F(+w)/P_R(-w) = exp((w-DeltaF)/kT), and Jarzynski <exp(-w/kT)> = exp(-DeltaF/kT). MC-sampling the stochastic release (u2t u2t.1) generates a nonequilibrium ENSEMBLE whose statistics obey these identities.

## Bio mapping
Release probability = a thermodynamic "work" protocol; metabolic energy = the cost; ACh/temperature (hy8) sets kT. The existing stochastic-release subsystem (already solid/tested) is the physical engine.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
(1) THERMODYNAMICALLY CALIBRATED uncertainty: the MC predictive distribution obeys a Crooks-type detailed-balance relation, giving a CALIBRATION GUARANTEE (an analytic relation the empirical histogram must satisfy) rather than only post-hoc ECE. (2) The TUR gives a provable precision/energy Pareto frontier, and the FLUCTUATION-THEOREM-optimal release temperature maximizes bits-per-joule (a Landauer-optimal attention). Baseline: softmax-entropy + MC-dropout calibration in u2t. Predicted: lower ECE / higher OOD-AUROC AND a satisfied fluctuation-theorem test the baselines structurally cannot satisfy.

## Artifacts
A TUR CERTIFICATE (measured precision x cost >= bound, per head); a CROOKS-RELATION calibration MONITOR (the empirical forward/reverse work histograms must satisfy the identity within tolerance); an energy-optimal TEMPERATURE SCHEDULE.

## Proof obligation (#2 finite-sample reliability + #4 tail)
Assumptions: release is a Markov jump process with the modeled rates (structural/distributional). Statement: Crooks/Jarzynski hold => calibration relation; TUR bounds estimator variance. Verify: empirical fluctuation-theorem test (forward vs time-reversed trajectories); coverage back-test. Failure: non-Markov / rate misspecification. Fallback: report empirical ECE only, drop the analytic-guarantee claim; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~4.0. Relevance: release IS a stochastic-thermodynamic process. Risk: the discrete approximation breaks the FT identities -> verify with the FT test before claiming calibration.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — theory thrust (alien-artifact discipline; in ADDITION to the standard DoD: unit tests + e2e in eqyk + detailed logging + default-off toggle + docs):
1. THEORY NOTE committed under docs/theory/ with the derivation + an explicit PROOF-OBLIGATION table: assumptions / formal statement / verification method / failure conditions / rollback.
2. Math COMPILED TO RUNTIME ARTIFACTS (certificate / monitor / structure-preserving integrator / guard table). Naming math without a runtime artifact is NOT done.
3. FALSIFIABLE leapfrog hypothesis tested against the NAMED baseline comparator with statistics (74f.3). A null/negative result is a valid, publishable outcome — report it honestly; do not p-hack.
4. DETERMINISTIC CONSERVATIVE FALLBACK to the engineering baseline, with a verified trigger, so a failed mathematical bet never breaks training (budgeted mode).
5. Composition with other thrusts GATED by the timescale-separation certificate (0642.10); record the timescale-separation statement.
NOT done until artifacts verify, the proof obligation holds (or is honestly reported failed), and the fallback is tested.

title: Thrust B — Ultrametric/RSB Hierarchical Associative Memory (hierarchical exponential capacity)
description:
# Thrust B — Ultrametric / Replica-Symmetry-Breaking Memory

## Math families
Parisi RSB ultrametricity + p-adic analysis (ultrametric coordinates) + modern Hopfield/associative-memory theory. 2-3 families, synergistic in the hierarchical-memory cluster.

## The mathematics
In Parisi's RSB solution of mean-field spin glasses, pure states are organized ULTRAMETRICALLY: the overlap matrix has a hierarchical (tree) structure, and distances satisfy the strong triangle inequality d(x,z) <= max(d(x,y),d(y,z)). p-adic numbers are the canonical coordinate system for ultrametric spaces (|.|_p is ultrametric). Modern Hopfield networks (Ramsauer et al.) show attention IS associative memory with exponential capacity — but FLAT (one level). We engineer the fast-weight energy landscape so its attractors nest ULTRAMETRICALLY (category -> subcategory -> instance), retrieved by a p-adic LCP kernel sim(q,k) ~ alpha^{LCP_p(q,k)}.

## Bio mapping
Fast-weight memory = hierarchical attractor net; VESICLE DEPLETION = coarse-to-fine hierarchical SEARCH (deplete the coarse attractor, descend to finer basins) — a literal gradient flow DOWN the ultrametric tree; septin lateral inhibition shapes basin boundaries.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
Hierarchical exponential-capacity memory with PROVABLE coarse-to-fine retrieval: retrieve the correct CATEGORY even when the instance is ambiguous/corrupted, with graceful degradation down the tree — beating flat modern-Hopfield/standard attention on compositional and noisy hierarchical recall. Baseline: standard attention + flat modern-Hopfield retrieval on the same task. Predicted: higher recall@category under corruption, and capacity scaling that matches the RSB prediction.

## Artifacts
A p-adic/ultrametric retrieval KERNEL (toggle); a CAPACITY CERTIFICATE (number of storable hierarchical memories vs ranks, from RSB theory); a retrieval-as-tree-gradient-flow MONITOR (energy descends levels); a basin/ultrametricity diagnostic (overlap-matrix tree-ness score).

## Proof obligation
Assumptions: landscape is ultrametric (verify via overlap-matrix tree-ness). Statement: capacity bound; depletion implements ultrametric descent. Verify: tree-ness score; recall-under-corruption back-test; capacity curve fit. Failure: landscape not ultrametric. Fallback: flat modern-Hopfield retrieval; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~3.75. Relevance: hierarchical memory is the project's stated theme. Risk: enforcing ultrametricity may cost capacity -> measure the tree-ness/capacity tradeoff explicitly.

title: Thrust C — Free-Probabilistic & Topological Structural Plasticity (spectrally-safe, topology-triggered NAS)
description:
# Thrust C — Free Probability + Persistent Homology + Optimal Transport for Structural Plasticity

## Math families
Free probability / random matrix theory + topological data analysis (persistent homology) + optimal transport. 3 families, each a DISTINCT role (birth conditioning / growth trigger / merge), so composition is justified.

## The mathematics
- FREE PROBABILITY: when you split/clone an expert with noise, the singular spectrum of the pair is a FREE CONVOLUTION of the parent spectrum with the noise spectrum; free probability predicts and lets us CONTROL the result so children are well-conditioned (bounded condition number kappa) by construction.
- PERSISTENT HOMOLOGY: the routing/activation point cloud has a shape; its persistence diagram reveals HOLES = regions of input space with no expert coverage. A high-persistence H0/H1 feature is a principled, noise-stable signal to GROW capacity THERE (bottleneck-stability theorem: features survive small perturbations).
- OPTIMAL TRANSPORT: merging two experts = the WASSERSTEIN BARYCENTER of their weight/routing distributions, the function-preserving merge (vs naive averaging).

## Bio mapping
Neurogenesis trigger = topological hole; birth = spectrally-controlled clone; apoptosis/merge = OT barycenter. This replaces the heuristic health=fatigue*energy thresholds in uta (uta) with principled signals.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
NAS-during-training driven by the TOPOLOGY of the representation manifold, with spectrally-safe births (no loss spikes, bounded kappa) and OT-optimal merges, beats heuristic-threshold MoE growth at equal FLOPs on coverage (dead-expert fraction), conditioning, and final loss. Baseline: uta health-threshold lifecycle (uta.1). Predicted: fewer dead experts + lower loss at equal compute, with smaller post-event loss spikes.

## Artifacts
A spectral-conditioning CERTIFICATE for new experts (kappa bound via free convolution); a PERSISTENCE-DIAGRAM MONITOR of the routing manifold (significance-thresholded by bottleneck stability); an OT-MERGE transport-plan certificate.

## Proof obligation (#36 topological stability + free-conv prediction + OT optimality)
Assumptions: manifold densely sampled; noise spectrum known. Statement: free-conv predicts child spectrum; bottleneck stability => genuine features; OT barycenter is the min-cost merge. Verify: spectrum prediction vs measured; diagram-stability under data perturbation; barycenter cost. Failure: sparse sampling / wrong metric. Fallback: heuristic uta lifecycle; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~3.0. Relevance: structural plasticity is intrinsically spectral+combinatorial. Risk: persistent homology cost -> use sparse/witness complexes; cap dimension.

title: Thrust H — Tropical/Idempotent Geometric Skeleton (exact interpretability + certified robustness)
description:
# Thrust H — Tropical Geometry of Attention & Routing

## Math families
Tropical (max-plus / min-plus) algebra + polyhedral geometry. 1-2 families; pairs with barrier/Lipschitz for robustness.

## The mathematics
In the low-temperature limit, softmax -> hardmax and attention/routing becomes a TROPICAL RATIONAL MAP: y = max_j (q.k_j + v_j) in the (max,+) semiring. Such maps are PIECEWISE LINEAR with POLYHEDRAL decision regions; the active token/expert at an input is a VERTEX of a Newton polytope. Tropical maps have exact, computable Lipschitz constants (the max-plus "slope").

## Bio mapping
Attention + septin lateral inhibition + vesicle depletion, near the low-temperature limit, is approximately tropical; depletion is a HISTORY-DEPENDENT tropical scaling (a "use tax" shifting the max). The stochastic temperature / septin sharpening ANNEALS between the soft (differentiable) and hard (tropical) regimes.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
A transformer with a tropical SKELETON is simultaneously (1) EXACTLY interpretable — its decision regions are polyhedra and each prediction has a Newton-polytope "fingerprint" of which inputs were active — and (2) CERTIFIABLY robust — a closed-form tropical Lipschitz bound certifies output stability to input perturbations — while the bio-dynamics provide adaptive, content-dependent modulation. Baseline: post-hoc attention-rollout interpretability + empirical robustness. Predicted: exact (not approximate) attributions + a non-vacuous certified-robustness radius.

## Artifacts
A tropical-REGION certificate (which polytope/vertex is active for an input); a tropical-LIPSCHITZ robustness certificate (per-layer slope -> certified radius); an interpretability FINGERPRINT (active-vertex set) MONITOR; a soft<->tropical anneal schedule.

## Proof obligation
Assumptions: near-low-temperature regime (measure the softmax entropy/gap). Statement: tropical map is PL with the stated Lipschitz constant; active vertex is exact. Verify: compare tropical prediction to the soft model as temperature->0; certified-radius back-test vs adversarial perturbation. Failure: high-temperature (soft) regime where tropical bound is loose. Fallback: report bound as a soft approximation; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~3.0. Relevance: attention is near-tropical; high interpretability/robustness EV. Risk: bound vacuous if too soft -> gate certificate on measured temperature.

title: Thrust D — Gauge-Theoretic Multi-Timescale Consolidation (forgetting = curvature; geodesic schedules)
description:
# Thrust D — Gauge Theory + Information Geometry of Consolidation

## Math families
Principal-bundle gauge theory (connection, holonomy, curvature) + information geometry (Fisher metric, natural gradient). 2 families, synergistic.

## The mathematics
The fast/slow weight split (W_total = W_slow + g(C)W_fast) has a GAUGE SYMMETRY: many (W_fast,W_slow) decompositions give the same W_total. Consolidation (fast->slow) is PARALLEL TRANSPORT along a CONNECTION A on the weight bundle; CaMKII/PP1/BDNF are components of A. The CURVATURE F = dA + A^A measures path-dependence of memory transport; HOLONOMY around a closed task-loop measures CATASTROPHIC FORGETTING. Information geometry supplies the natural metric (Fisher) so transport is the NATURAL GRADIENT.

## Bio mapping
Metaplasticity (sax) + sleep consolidation (cel) become the connection + its transport; surprise = curvature; the optimal consolidation schedule is a GEODESIC in the Fisher metric.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
A coordinate-free consolidation law gives a PROVABLE continual-learning bound: forgetting over a task sequence <= integral of curvature along the path (a holonomy bound), and Fisher-geodesic consolidation schedules minimize it. Baseline: cel replay/consolidation + EWC-style regularization. Predicted: less forgetting at equal compute on the continual-learning benchmark, with the measured holonomy tracking the measured forgetting.

## Artifacts
A CURVATURE MONITOR (forgetting early-warning); a Fisher-natural-gradient CONSOLIDATION step; a HOLONOMY ledger (closed-loop forgetting measurement); a gauge-invariance guard (W_total unchanged under regauge — a behavior-preservation certificate).

## Proof obligation (#5/#6 transport coherence + #15)
Assumptions: bundle/connection well-defined; Fisher estimable. Statement: forgetting <= holonomy bound; regauge preserves W_total. Verify: holonomy-vs-forgetting correlation; gauge-invariance test. Failure: Fisher ill-conditioned. Fallback: diagonal/EWC approximation; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~2.4 (high effort). Relevance: multi-timescale memory is intrinsically a transport problem. Risk: full curvature is expensive -> use low-rank/diagonal Fisher; treat as exploratory.

title: Thrust G — Operadic/Sheaf-Theoretic Compositional Binding (provable systematic generalization)
description:
# Thrust G — Operads + Sheaf Cohomology for the Binding Problem

## Math families
Operads / monoidal categories + sheaf theory (sections, gluing, sheaf Laplacian) + cohomological obstruction. Local-to-global cluster (sheaf + category synergistic ++).

## The mathematics
Compositional binding ("the RED cube and the BLUE sphere" — who is red?) is a LOCAL-TO-GLOBAL consistency problem. Model the token graph as a cellular sheaf: each token carries a stalk (its feature/binding vector), each edge a restriction map; a globally CONSISTENT binding is a GLOBAL SECTION; an inconsistent one is a nonzero H^1 (cohomological OBSTRUCTION). The Rab/SNARE key-lock routing is the OPERADIC composition rule (cargo codes compose like operad operations). The SHEAF LAPLACIAN L = delta^T delta diffuses toward consistency; ker(L) = global sections.

## Bio mapping
Rab/SNARE routing (existing yxq idea) = operadic binding; septin/lateral inhibition = sharpening sections; a sheaf-Laplacian "consistency diffusion" sublayer enforces local-to-global agreement.

## Leapfrog hypothesis (falsifiable)
A sheaf-structured transformer DETECTS binding inconsistencies (nonzero cohomology) and REPAIRS them (sheaf-Laplacian diffusion to the nearest global section), giving provable SYSTEMATIC COMPOSITIONAL GENERALIZATION — a fundamental, known failure mode of vanilla transformers. Baseline: standard transformer on SCAN/COGS-style systematic-generalization splits. Predicted: higher exact-match on held-out compositions + a consistency certificate (H^1=0) on correct outputs.

## Artifacts
A sheaf-consistency MONITOR (H^1 obstruction detector / Laplacian spectral gap); a sheaf-Laplacian CONSISTENCY-DIFFUSION layer (toggle); a binding CERTIFICATE (global section exists).

## Proof obligation (#5 local-to-global coherence)
Assumptions: a meaningful cover/edge structure exists (multi-token binding). Statement: H^1=0 iff a consistent global binding exists; Laplacian diffusion converges to ker(L). Verify: cocycle witness ledger; SCAN/COGS back-test; ablation of the diffusion layer. Failure: no multi-view structure (single-token). Fallback: standard routing; flag.

## EV / relevance / risk
EV ~2.0 (high impact, lower confidence). Relevance: addresses transformers' core compositionality weakness. Risk: sheaf overhead w/o multi-view signal -> gate on tasks with genuine binding structure; exploratory.

title: Leapfrog Theory — overview, EV ranking & reading order (START HERE)
description:
## Purpose
Entry point for the theory program (filter: label theory). The synaptic transformer = a stochastic, gauge-covariant, metriplectic neural SDE on a fiber bundle with separated timescales; each thrust applies the right esoteric math to one stratum.

## Reading order (by EV and dependency)
1. Thrust A (0642.1) Metriplectic/GENERIC — stability+conservation by construction (KEYSTONE, do first).
2. Thrust F (0642.2) Singular perturbation/cusp — certified memory + the TIMESCALE-SEPARATION backbone that licenses composing everything else.
3. The timescale-separation certificate (composition keystone) + the unified-foundation capstone.
4. Thrust E (thermo UQ), B (ultrametric memory), then C (topological NAS), H (tropical), D (gauge), G (sheaf).

## Discipline reminder
Every thrust: compile math -> runtime artifacts; falsifiable hypothesis vs a named baseline (with 74f stats); proof obligations; and a DETERMINISTIC FALLBACK to the engineering baseline so a failed bet never breaks training. Each ships behind a default-off toggle (hm4.7).

## Acceptance
This note kept current as thrusts land; cross-links to the per-thrust theory notes under docs/theory/.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Starting work on distributed population eval harness.

Agent Mail is not available in this environment (BEADS_AGENT_MAIL_URL not set), so I’ll use bd comments for coordination notes.

Plan:
- Implement torchrun/DDP controller+worker loop for evaluating CMA-ES populations with device pinning (rank -> cuda:local_rank).
- Add robust error handling (timeouts/retry) so a bad candidate doesn’t hang the run.
- Provide a CPU-only fallback mode for local testing.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Implemented distributed population evaluation harness in `scripts/tune_bio_params.py`.

What’s in place:
- `optimize --distributed` runs under `torchrun`: rank0 runs CMA-ES controller, workers evaluate assigned candidates and `all_gather` fitness.
- Device pinning: in CUDA mode, each rank uses `cuda:{LOCAL_RANK}` (and calls `torch.cuda.set_device(LOCAL_RANK)` before init).

title: Security: Replace calculator eval() with safe AST evaluator
close_reason: Completed

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Found unsafe use of Python eval() in bio_inspired_nanochat/engine.py calculator tool. Even with __builtins__ removed, eval() can be exploited via object graph tricks. Replace with AST-based evaluator that only allows numeric ops (+-*/%//, parens, unary +/-) and a single safe string op: literal_string.count(literal_string). Keep existing timeout guard.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Fixed: replaced eval()-based calculator in bio_inspired_nanochat/engine.py with AST-based evaluator (numeric ops + literal str.count only), keeping SIGALRM timeout guard. Added regression tests in tests/test_engine.py.

title: Author the unified canonical differentiable presyn release function (additive)
description:
S1+S2 of 8j9.2. Add a new method on SynapticPresyn implementing the CANONICAL faithful equations on the TOP-K differentiable structure, WITHOUT touching the live release()/forward() yet (additive, low-risk).

Canonical eqs (from forward()): Hill Syt fast=C/(C+syt_fast_kd), slow=C/(C+syt_slow_kd), syt=.7fast+.3slow; BUF calcium ODE (rho_c=exp(-1/tau_c), alpha_buf_on/off); fuse_base=sigmoid(3*syt+2*pr-2*(cl+complexin_bias)); per-edge release rr=fuse_base*d_bilin*rrp_refill with W_eff=min(P,RRP) row-sum scale; energy ODE + qamp=sigmoid(q_beta*(e-.5))*qmax; septin barrier -barrier_strength*dist using REAL key positions from idx. MUST be differentiable w.r.t. drive/logits. Preserve stochastic STE path + endocytosis DELAY queue + EMA normalization.

Tests (this subtask): (a) equivalence to forward() on the K=T full-attention case within atol; (b) grad flows to drive (autograd.grad nonzero, finite); (c) all state buffers updated & finite; (d) W_eff<=RRP invariant. Detailed logging via run_logging.

Acceptance: new function exists, additive (live path unchanged), golden-equivalent to forward() at K=T, differentiable. Blocks the migration subtask.
close_reason: Canonical differentiable presyn fn authored + property-tested (additive); commit 62d79cc

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
DONE (commit 62d79cc) by TurquoiseFinch.

Delivered: SynapticPresyn.release_canonical + _faithful_release_prob — additive (live path still calls release()), differentiable w.r.t. drive, all features preserved (BUF ODE now active, Doc2 preserved, septin barrier, stochastic STE, endo DELAY queue, EMA norm). 13 property/differentiability tests pass; full suite 134 passed.

SCOPE CLARIFICATION (important for ukxt + 8j9.4): the original acceptance said 'golden-equivalent to forward() at K=T'. Deep analysis showed the canonical is a DELIBERATE REFORMULATION, not byte-equal to forward(): forward() uses per-QUERY-row RRP with min(P,RRP) row-scaling and normalized [0,1] state scales; the canonical uses per-KEY vesicle-unit RRP (release()'s live structure) where p*RRP is naturally <= RRP. These are different state bookkeeping for the SAME faithful equation forms. So byte-equivalence to old forward() is NOT the right target — instead:
- 8j9.4 should lock the CANONICAL's golden semantics (snapshot release_canonical outputs), not assert equivalence to the soon-to-be-removed forward().
- ukxt migrates the live attention path to release_canonical AND replaces/reconciles forward() so they no longer diverge (forward becomes the full-attention K=T view of the canonical, or is removed).

DECISIONS LOCKED for ukxt: RRP=per-key vesicle units; output convention=return e for lambda_loge*log(eps+e) (barrier folded into e via exp(-barrier*dist)); amplitude=energy-qamp (AMP superseded, remove in or4t); recurrence detached (yw9 owns differentiable kinetics).

title: Epic — Evaluation Rigor & the Meta-Science of Bio-Inspiration
description:
# Epic — Evaluation Rigor & the Meta-Science of Bio-Inspiration

## Why this matters (overarching goal: USEFUL, publishable science)
Per the planning corpus, the BIGGEST practical blocker is not the optimizer but the EVALUATION: the CMA-ES proxy was a documented no-go (signal 1.46e-4 vs noise 2.1e-3, ~10-15x noise-dominated), NIAH is a hardcoded `None`, there is no statistical testing anywhere, and eval_matrix trains under a crude loop that does not match the real recipe (a confound). The deepest scientific question — "does biological plausibility actually help, which mechanisms, and are they redundant?" — can only be answered with rigorous, recipe-faithful, statistically-tested evaluation. This epic is what turns the project from a demo into science.

## Current reality (ground truth)
- `eval_matrix.py:716` `niah_acc=None`; zero t-tests/CIs/bootstrap in code; `eval_matrix.py:608` train loop has no LR schedule/grad-clip/Muon and is single-process-only.
- CMA-ES proxy = mean last-10 TRAIN loss on a 2-layer toy copy task.

## What to build / Approach
Fix the eval proxy (held-out metric, multi-seed, harder task with delimiters) so it can drive optimization; implement NIAH; a statistical layer (paired t/Wilcoxon, bootstrap CIs, multi-seed aggregation); a recipe-faithful matrix (evaluate base_train checkpoints, not a different inner loop); the redundancy/saturation meta-experiment; a bio scaling-law study; and capability-specific metrics (ECE/AUROC, forgetting-rate, routing-Gini).

## Acceptance criteria
- Proxy signal > 3σ over noise; NIAH live; every headline comparison ships with paired stats + CIs; a written answer to "does bio help, which parts, redundant?".

Unblocks: hea. Relates: existing Epic gzm (bio-vs-vanilla eval).
acceptance_criteria: Eval proxy signal > 3 sigma over noise; NIAH live; every headline comparison ships paired stats + bootstrap CIs over multi-seed runs; a written answer to 'does bio help, which parts, are they redundant?'
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Fix the evaluation/CMA-ES proxy signal (the documented blocker)
description:
## Goal (the #1 practical blocker)
Make the optimization/eval objective informative. The real Phase-1 CMA-ES run was a NO-GO: best improvement ~1.46e-4 vs seed-noise ~2.10e-3 (signal ~10-15x below noise), OAT deltas all O(1e-4). The objective — mean last-10 TRAIN loss on a 2-layer toy copy task, single fixed seed reused every generation — is noise-dominated and not held-out.

## Fixes
- Optimize a HELD-OUT metric (val BPB on a fixed slice, or a harder task with explicit delimiters/distractors).
- AVERAGE over >=3 seeds per candidate (so objective noise actually shrinks).
- Larger/longer proxy or learning-curve extrapolation tuned to give signal > 3σ over noise.

## Acceptance
A documented proxy where a known-good vs known-bad config differ by > 3σ; readiness gate met (>=0.5% relative improvement detectable on >=1 seed).

Blocks: hea (all search). Relates: existing CMA-ES Epic 0xd.
notes: Infrastructure + root-cause fix DELIVERED (commit 0e55930), strict >3sigma validation PENDING (scale-bound -> hea.1). Root cause found: the candidate eval loop never reset per-sequence plasticity state, so synaptic candidates accumulated eligibility/fast-weight state and diverged to NaN->PENALTY_LOSS (verified: default config NaNs without per-batch reset, finite with it). Implemented all prescribed fixes: (1) reset_sequence_state per batch in evaluate_candidate_detailed; (2) HELD-OUT objective on a fixed set (generate_batch gained seed; CandidateEvalResult.objective prefers held_out); (3) multi_seed_objective averages over >=3 seeds; (4) readiness_from_objectives/proxy_signal_check measure good-vs-bad separation in seed-noise sigma + paired test (eval_stats). 6 tests (test_tune_proxy.py). Demonstration (small CPU proxy, 3 seeds, presyn good vs degraded): 45.9% relative improvement (>=0.5% rel readiness sub-gate MET) but degraded config has large seed variance (unstable), so sigma-separation=0.6 (strict >3sigma NOT met at this scale). The >3sigma gate is now MEASURABLE; reaching it needs more seeds / a larger-longer proxy, which is hea.1's scope. NOT closing until >3sigma is demonstrated.

title: Implement Needle-in-a-Haystack long-context benchmark
description:
## Goal
NIAH is currently a hardcoded `None` (`eval_matrix.py:716`) and "(planned)" in the eval doc, yet it is the key test for the fast-weight / long-context claim. Implement it for real at multiple context lengths (4k/8k+), with distractors, wired into eval_matrix and the metrics schema.

## Acceptance
NIAH returns real accuracy-by-length; integrated into eval_matrix + RunRecord; used in bio-vs-vanilla.

Relates: existing bead v7c, sax working-memory suite.
close_reason: Implemented NIAH for real: synthetic_tasks.retrieval_accuracy + niah_accuracy_by_length (length × depth sweep, per-batch fast-weight reset, returns by_length curve + overall). eval_matrix now computes niah_acc per preset (was hardcoded None) into summary.csv, consumable by the 74f.3 stats layer for bio-vs-vanilla. metrics_schema already registers niah_accuracy (HIGHER_BETTER). 6 tests incl. perfect-oracle=1.0 end-to-end + both model types. README + eval_benchmark_matrix.md updated (planned->implemented). Full suite 307 passed.

title: Statistical testing layer (paired tests, bootstrap CIs, multi-seed aggregation)
description:
## Goal
There is currently ZERO statistical testing in code. Add a layer that aggregates across seeds and computes paired t / Wilcoxon tests and bootstrap CIs on bio-vs-vanilla deltas (matched seeds), so every headline comparison ships with significance + uncertainty.

## Acceptance
Given matrix outputs, produces per-preset mean ± 95% CI and paired significance vs vanilla; used by all comparison tasks (yw9 ablate, uta eval, sax, etc.).
close_reason: Implemented bio_inspired_nanochat/eval_stats.py: multi-seed aggregation (Student-t 95% CI), paired t-test + Wilcoxon signed-rank (exact n<=18 / normal-approx above) + paired bootstrap CI + Cohen's d_z, and direction-aware compare_matrix() reusing metrics_schema, plus a CLI over eval_matrix summary.csv (per-preset mean+/-CI + BETTER/WORSE* verdict). Pure-numpy (no scipy) via regularized incomplete beta; validated vs known t-quantiles, two-sided p-values, and exact Wilcoxon. 27 tests. Acceptance met: per-preset mean +/- 95% CI and paired significance vs baseline; importable by all comparison tasks (uta.7, yw9.6, cel.4, 74f.5/6, 0642.*). Full suite 301 passed.

title: Recipe-faithful bio-vs-vanilla matrix (evaluate real checkpoints)
description:
## Goal
Remove the recipe-mismatch confound. `eval_matrix.py:608` trains with a crude loop (no LR schedule, no grad clip, no Muon, single-process). Instead, EVALUATE checkpoints produced by the real `base_train` recipe (or replicate the recipe exactly), so bio-vs-vanilla numbers reflect the real training dynamics.

## Acceptance
Matrix consumes base_train checkpoints (or a recipe-identical loop); documented that the confound is eliminated; multi-process capable.

title: Redundancy/saturation meta-experiment across bio mechanisms
description:
## Goal
Test the predictions doc's key worry: BDNF, CaMKII/PP1, energy metabolism, and NeuroScore may all be controlling the SAME underlying phenomenon, so "more biology" could give diminishing or negative returns. Use the ablation matrix + correlation/mediation analysis to measure mechanism overlap and interaction.

## Acceptance
A written analysis: which mechanisms are independent vs redundant, with evidence; informs which to keep/cut. A genuinely publishable result either way.

title: Bio scaling-law study (does biology shift the compute Pareto frontier?)
description:
## Goal
Run a scaling study (vary depth/width/tokens) for bio vs vanilla and fit loss-vs-compute curves. The high-impact question: do bio mechanisms change the scaling EXPONENT or just the offset — i.e., do they shift the Pareto frontier?

## Acceptance
Fitted scaling curves with CIs for bio vs vanilla; a clear statement of whether bio helps more/less at scale. A flagship scientific result if positive.

Depends on: 74f stats + recipe-faithful matrix.

title: Wire capability-specific metrics into the matrix
description:
## Goal
Operationalize the vague claims into concrete metrics in the matrix: ECE/AUROC (uncertainty, for u2t), forgetting-rate (continual, for cel), routing-Gini/dead-expert-fraction (structural, for uta), recall-by-length (memory, for sax). Each bio claim must map to a measurable number up front.

## Acceptance
Each capability metric implemented + emitted by eval_matrix; referenced by the owning epic's eval task.
