title: Epic — Truth & Integrity: close the claim↔code gap
description:
# Epic — Truth & Integrity: Close the Claim↔Code Gap

## Why this matters (overarching goal: TRUSTWORTHY foundation)
A deep code investigation (June 2026) found a large gap between what the README/marketing claims and what the code actually computes. Before we can be ambitious, the substrate must do what it says. This epic makes the project HONEST and gives every downstream epic a single, trustworthy source of truth to build on. Scientific credibility (and any future publication) depends on it.

## Current reality (ground truth, with evidence)
- TWO disjoint presynaptic implementations exist in `synaptic.py`. The LIVE path used by the model is `SynapticPresyn.release()` (sigmoid-based, top-k edges). The README's governing equations (Hill `Syt(C)=C/(C+Kd)`, `W_eff=min(P,RRP)`) actually describe `SynapticPresyn.forward()` (`synaptic.py:466`), which is `@torch.no_grad()` and ONLY called by `neuroviz.py` — i.e. the biologically-faithful, equation-matching code is decorative/dead.
- README "48-parameter genome" is inflated: real `SynapticConfig` has ~70 fields, several of which are dead (`enabled`, `camkii_down`, `router_sim_threshold`, `native_presyn`, `native_plasticity` are read by nothing).
- README equations for CaMKII/PP1 (`σ(CaMKII−PP1)`) and `W_eff=min(P,RRP)` are literally false for the live path.

## What to build / Approach
Audit every public claim, reconcile code to ONE source of truth, and rewrite docs to match reality (clearly separating SHIPPING vs ASPIRATIONAL). The flagship deliverable is unifying the two presyn implementations into a single differentiable function that IS the documented one — which also unblocks the Differentiable-Dynamics and Kernels epics.

## Acceptance criteria
- A `CLAIMS_AUDIT.md` maps every README claim → {SOLID|PARTIAL|ASPIRATIONAL} with file:line evidence.
- The presyn implementations are unified (one differentiable function, golden-tested), or the divergence is explicitly documented and gated.
- README/docs contain no claim contradicted by code; dead config fields are pruned or wired.
- All child tasks closed.

Serves: robustness, credibility, and unblocks yw9 + jyb.
acceptance_criteria: CLAIMS_AUDIT.md maps every claim->status with evidence; the two presyn impls are unified (one differentiable, golden-tested) or divergence is documented+gated; no doc claim contradicted by code; dead config pruned/wired; all child tasks closed.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.
close_reason: All 8 children closed. Acceptance met: CLAIMS_AUDIT.md maps every claim->status w/ evidence (8j9.1, kept current); presyn unified into one differentiable source of truth + golden-locked (8j9.2 + 8j9.4); param census replaces the 48-genome narrative, 6 dead fields pruned so all 70 are live (8j9.6 + 8j9.5); NeuroScore wired into the lifecycle (de5l) and the router lateral-inhibition fixed to be effective (fkkc); README reconciled with a ship/aspirational status legend, zero present-tense claims contradicted by code (8j9.3). Substrate is now honest.

title: Claims audit: map every README claim to code status
description:
## Goal
Produce `CLAIMS_AUDIT.md` mapping every public claim (README, planning docs) to {SOLID|PARTIAL|ASPIRATIONAL} with file:line evidence. This is the reference all other Truth tasks act on.

## Known gaps to record (from June-2026 audit)
- "Online Hebbian learning" — inert at train time (`synaptic.py:861`).
- README presyn equations describe DEAD `forward()` (`synaptic.py:466`), not live `release()`.
- "Bistable CaMKII/PP1 latch" — no bistability; PP1 not in gate (`synaptic.py:720`).
- "48-parameter genome" — ~70 fields, several dead.
- "Three kernel backends / 90% util" — headline kernels are dead/test-only/unbuilt.
- "NeuroScore evolutionary credit assignment" — never consumed by lifecycle.
- "Bank-account economy (taxation/bankruptcy/IPO)" — does not exist.

## Acceptance
A committed audit table; each row has claim, status, evidence, and a linked bead that closes the gap (or an explicit "won't fix / reframed" note).
close_reason: CLAIMS_AUDIT.md committed with verified statuses + linked closing beads

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: DONE by TurquoiseFinch. CLAIMS_AUDIT.md committed: ~35 claims mapped to SOLID/PARTIAL/ASPIRATIONAL with file:line evidence + linked closing beads. Agent draft re-verified by hand (corrected several mis-ratings). Recorded the recently-closed wins (vg9.2 Hebbian, 2gjl/ukxt presyn, x6z4 tau_c). Filed de5l (NeuroScore unwired) + fkkc (router contrastive) for gaps lacking a bead. Blocks 8j9.6 + 8j9.3.

title: Unify the two presynaptic implementations into one differentiable source of truth
description:
## Goal (FLAGSHIP foundation)
Collapse `SynapticPresyn.release()` (live, sigmoid/top-k, differentiable bias) and `SynapticPresyn.forward()` (faithful Hill/buffer ODE, `@no_grad`, dead) into ONE canonical, differentiable function that the model runs AND the docs describe AND the kernels implement.

## Why
Today the biologically-faithful equations are decorative and the live path diverges from them (sigmoid vs Hill `Syt(C)=C/(C+Kd)`; `rel=p*rrp` vs `W_eff=min(P,RRP)`; BUF unused in `release()`). This split is the root cause of most claim↔code gaps and blocks differentiable dynamics (yw9) and a correct fused kernel (jyb), whose parity tests currently validate the dead equation.

## Approach
Choose the canonical formulation (prefer the faithful, differentiable one), unify Set-A/Set-B parameter names, keep the top-k edge selection for efficiency, ensure it is differentiable w.r.t. inputs (and, for yw9, w.r.t. kinetics). Provide a single reference impl that golden tests + Triton + Rust all target.

## Acceptance
One function; README equations match it exactly; `release`/`forward` no longer diverge; golden test locks semantics; downstream kernels/diff build on it.
design: SCOPE BOUNDARY (to avoid overlap with the Differentiable Dynamics epic yw9): this task delivers ONE canonical presyn function that (a) matches the documented equations, (b) is forward-correct and golden-tested (8j9.4), and (c) is differentiable w.r.t. its INPUTS/bias — i.e. parity with what today's release() already does into the attention logits. Making the STATE RECURRENCE (calcium/RRP/energy) differentiable w.r.t. KINETICS via BPTT/associative-scan is the SEPARATE flagship epic yw9, which DEPENDS on this. Keep the top-k edge selection for efficiency; unify Set-A (release) and Set-B (kernel/forward) parameter names into one set; preserve ALL existing behavior/features (do not drop the buffer ODE, endocytosis delay queue, Doc2 term, stochastic path, or septin barrier) — fold them into the single function.
close_reason: Presyn unification complete: canonical differentiable release (2gjl), live migration (ukxt), tau_c unification (x6z4), flex migration (s3w9), legacy deletion (qcj7), dead-param removal (or4t). One faithful release path; release/forward no longer diverge; README + tune + kernels consistent.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
DESIGN (TurquoiseFinch, after deep read of release() L289-476 and forward() L478-609).

== DIVERGENCE CATALOG (exact) ==
1. Release prob: release()=_mix_prob sigmoids (syt1_slope=8,syt7_slope=3,doc2_gain=.08,cpx gate); forward()=Hill fast=C/(C+syt_fast_kd=.4), slow=C/(C+syt_slow_kd=1.0), syt=.7fast+.3slow.
2. Release amount: release() rel=p*rrp (UNBOUNDED by RRP except via clamp); forward() W_eff=min(P,RRP) via row-sum scale=min(1,rrp/row_sum).
3. Calcium: release() c=tau_c(.85)*c+alpha_c(.55)*softplus(drive), NO BUF; forward() full buffer ODE c_next=rho_c*c+alpha_ca*influx-alpha_buf_on*c*(1-buf)+alpha_buf_off*buf with buf_next ODE. rho_c=exp(-1/tau_c) vs raw tau_c.
4. AMPA: release() separate AMP state (amp_load/amp_leak); forward() qamp=sigmoid(q_beta*(e-.5))*qmax from energy.
5. Topology: release() top-k (B,H,T,K) parallel; forward() full (B,H,T,T) sequential loop (but rows are independent given init state+logits -> parallelizable).
6. Barrier: release() none; forward() -barrier_strength(.1)*dist (septin). release() also returns e/(ema_e) EMA-normalized; forward() returns log(rel*qamp)-barrier directly.
7. Stochastic: release() has binomial STE (stochastic_train_frac); forward() deterministic.
8. Endo queue: release() has DELAY queue (endo_delay=3,rec_rate); forward() uses alpha_refill direct.
9. Params: Set-A (alpha_c,syt1/7_slope,doc2_gain,nsf_recover,prime_rate,rec_rate,amp_load/leak,energy_fill/use) vs Set-B (alpha_ca,syt_fast/slow_kd,alpha_buf_on/off,alpha_prime/refill/unprime,energy_in/cost_rel/cost_pump,q_beta,qmax).

== CANONICAL DECISION (per bead: prefer faithful + differentiable) ==
Canonical = forward()'s faithful equations (Hill Syt, BUF ODE, W_eff=min(P,RRP), energy-qamp, septin barrier) ported onto release()'s TOP-K differentiable structure. Keep: top-k edge selection, differentiability w.r.t. drive/logits, stochastic STE path, endocytosis DELAY queue, EMA normalization, loge_bias_clamp at the attention boundary. The result must be ONE function the live attention path calls AND the kernels target AND README describes.

== SAFE MIGRATION (golden-gated; do NOT big-bang the live engine) ==
S1 (additive): author unified _release_canonical(state,drive,idx,...) implementing the canonical eqs on top-k; ZERO change to live path yet.
S2: golden-lock it vs forward() on the K=T full-attention case (8j9.4 owns the formal golden suite; add an equivalence test here). + differentiability test (grad flows to drive).
S3: param unification Set-A/Set-B -> one set; update Rust Config::from_py field reads + tune scripts + checkpoints note (coordinate w/ vg9.6).
S4: migrate live attention path (synaptic.py:1161,1186) to the canonical fn; remove divergent bodies; validate a tiny end-to-end train step (loss finite, no NaN, grads flow).
S5: README equations updated to match exactly.

RISK: S4 changes live training dynamics -> requires a smoke train-run gate before close. S1-S2 are low-risk/additive and are the right first deliverable.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Migration impact note (ukxt fresh-eyes-3): switching the live path to the faithful exp calcium decay (rho_c=exp(-1/tau_c)) cut effective calcium memory ~7x vs legacy (half-life 4.26 -> 0.59 steps at default tau_c=0.85). Equation is correct/faithful; the default tau_c needs re-tuning for the canonical regime (tau_c~6.15 restores legacy memory). Tracked on or4t; validate via 4fw. Also: canonical equations verified EXACT vs hand-derivation (p=0.813148 to 6 digits).

title: Rewrite README and docs to match reality (ship vs aspirational)
description:
## Goal
Make every doc claim true. Separate SHIPPING from ASPIRATIONAL with explicit status badges; fix the false equations (CaMKII/PP1 gate, W_eff=min(P,RRP)); correct the parameter-count narrative (use the census from the param-census task); remove dead-feature implications.

## Why
Scientific credibility and onboarding both depend on docs that don't contradict the code. Aspirational claims belong in a clearly-labeled roadmap section, not as present-tense capabilities.

## Considerations
Keep the compelling framing (the "living fluid" metaphor is good) but tie each claim to a status. Respect the Contribution-Policy (no contributor mentions). Depends on the claims audit + param census.

## Acceptance
README/docs reviewed against `CLAIMS_AUDIT.md`; zero present-tense claims contradicted by code; equations match the unified presyn function.
close_reason: README reconciled against CLAIMS_AUDIT.md with a ✅/🚧/🔮 status legend. Fixed: 48->70 param narrative (census-backed) across all mentions; false CaMKII/PP1 Hebbian gate equation -> real σ(CaMKII-0.5)-0.3 slow-weight consolidation (PP1 not in gate); bistable latch marked roadmap; bank-account/bankruptcy/IPO reframed as metaphor for energy-metabolism+health lifecycle; kernel 'Production' -> landing targets (live path is PyTorch release_canonical); NeuroScore -> opt-in (de5l); stochastic/BDNF/dual-weights marked shipping. Zero present-tense claims contradicted by code; presyn equations match release_canonical/_faithful_release_prob.

title: Golden reference tests locking the canonical presyn dynamics
description:
## Goal
After unification, freeze the canonical presyn dynamics with golden artifacts (fixed seed, fixed inputs → expected state + bias tensors) so future refactors and kernel backends cannot silently change semantics.

## Why
There is currently NO Triton-vs-anything parity test, and Rust parity only covers the dead `forward()` equation. Golden tests are the contract the unified function and all backends must satisfy.

## Approach
Generate golden tensors from the canonical Python reference; assert Triton/Rust/Python agree to tight tolerance on the SAME equation; include state-conservation checks. Commit goldens under tests/.

## Acceptance
Golden suite passes for Python; backend parity tests (jyb) reuse the same goldens; CI runs them.
close_reason: Golden suite committed (tests/test_presyn_golden.py + tests/golden/presyn_canonical_v1.npz). Freezes release_canonical (4-step trajectory: e + full state + DELAY + EMA) and the _faithful_release_prob Hill formula over a fixed grid; adds physical-invariant + determinism checks. Self-contained npz stores inputs+expected outputs; jyb backend-parity reuses via load_golden(). Verified lock detects drift (tau_c 6->3 => max|Δe|=1.5e-2 vs 1e-6 tol). forward() intentionally not cross-asserted (different execution model). Unblocks eqyk.13, jyb.2. Full suite 229 passed.

title: Prune or wire dead SynapticConfig fields
description:
## Goal
Eliminate phantom config that inflates the "genome" and the CMA-ES search space with no-op dimensions.

## Dead fields found (read by nothing)
`enabled` (`synaptic.py:138`), `camkii_down`, `router_sim_threshold`, `native_presyn`, `native_plasticity`. (`native_genetics` IS wired; `native_metrics` partially.)

## Approach
For each: either WIRE it to the behavior it implies, or REMOVE it and migrate callers/tests. Do not silently keep no-op knobs. Coordinate with the param census so the count narrative stays accurate.

## Acceptance
Every remaining `SynapticConfig` field is read somewhere; removed fields have callers migrated; tests updated.
close_reason: Removed 6 dead SynapticConfig fields (enabled, camkii_down, router_sim_threshold, native_presyn, native_metrics, native_plasticity) + migrated all callers (verify_evolution, benchmark_flex, test_engine, test_rust_kernels, test_bdnf_metaplasticity). native_genetics retained (live dispatch). Census now 70 fields, 70 live/0 dead; test enforces zero-dead invariant. Proper wiring for the removed knobs is owned by sax.2 (CaMKII), fkkc (router contrastive), jyb.2 (live presyn kernel). Full suite 223 passed.

title: Accurate parameter census (replace the '48-parameter genome' narrative)
description:
## Goal
A precise, committed census of every tunable: name, default, where read, LIVE vs DEAD, HYPERPARAMETER vs LEARNED, and subsystem. Feeds the docs rewrite and the optimization search space (hea census-driven search).

## Why
README says "48-parameter genome"; the code has ~70 `SynapticConfig` fields with different live counts in `release()` (Set A) vs kernels (Set B), plus dead ones. The CMA-ES plan inherited this confusion. An accurate census prevents searching no-op dimensions.

## Acceptance
A machine-readable census (e.g., generated from introspection + grep) committed and referenced by docs and hea.
close_reason: Machine-generated census committed (docs/parameter_census.{json,md} + scripts/param_census.py); 76 fields, 70 live/6 dead, 10 CMA-ES-tuned. README count narrative corrected + linked. 7 unit tests incl. drift guard. Feeds 8j9.5 (prune) and 8j9.3 (docs).

title: Perf: Data pipeline throughput (dual 4090)
description:
Goal: maximize dataloader throughput to keep dual 4090s saturated.
Subtasks:

notes: Implemented a correctness fix so CUDA loader optimizations (pin_memory + non_blocking H2D copies) remain enabled when passing torch.device('cuda:*') instead of only the string 'cuda'. Also hardened small-file downloads (eval bundle/wordlists) with streaming + timeouts + atomic temp->replace under a file lock to avoid hangs/partial files.

title: Gate online plasticity off during eval (update_mem not wired to train_mode)
description:
## Issue (surfaced by vg9.2 fresh-eyes review)
SynapticLinear.forward has an `update_mem` parameter to control whether online Hebbian plasticity runs, but SynapticMLP.forward and SynapticExpert.forward call it WITHOUT passing update_mem (synaptic.py:1234, 1237, and the expert fc1/fc2) — so it defaults to True ALWAYS. Plasticity gating therefore relies solely on self.training + torch.is_grad_enabled().

evaluate_bpb (loss_eval.py:12) is decorated @torch.no_grad(), and base_train calls model.eval() before evaluation. With training=False + no_grad, run_plasticity = update_mem(True) and (not grad_enabled(True) or ...) = True. So EVALUATION mutates the model's Hebbian state (u_buf, v_buf, w_fast, w_slow, post.fast/slow/camkii/pp1/bdnf) on VALIDATION batches.

## Why it matters now
This is pre-existing (the original inference path ran under no_grad too), but vg9.2 made plasticity genuinely live, so the consequences are now material:
- Train/eval contamination: validation data adapts the model.
- Eval is NOT idempotent: running evaluate_bpb twice gives different numbers because the first run changed the weights.
- val_bpb is measured on a model that drifts during the eval loop itself.

## Fix
Wire an explicit no-plasticity signal down to SynapticLinear during evaluation. Options:
(a) Thread train_mode/update_mem from GPTSynaptic.forward(train_mode=...) -> blocks -> MLP/MoE -> SynapticLinear(update_mem=train_mode). The plumbing for train_mode already exists for the presyn path (synaptic.py:1161,1186) but stops short of the postsyn SynapticLinear.
(b) Simpler: in SynapticLinear, also require self.training for plasticity even under no_grad — i.e. run_plasticity = update_mem and self.training and (not grad or plasticity_during_training). Then model.eval() alone disables plasticity. NOTE this changes the legacy inference-time adaptation semantics, so confirm intent first.

## Acceptance
- evaluate_bpb does NOT mutate any plasticity buffer/weight (assert state unchanged across an eval pass).
- Eval is idempotent: two consecutive evaluate_bpb calls on the same data give identical bpb.
- Training-time plasticity (vg9.2) still runs.
- Unit test asserting no plasticity-state delta across a no_grad eval forward in model.eval() mode.

Relationship: surfaced by and depends conceptually on vg9.2 (training-inert gate fix). Sibling of vg9.4 (per-sequence reset of plasticity buffers).

title: Epic — Sleep, Consolidation & Continual Learning
description:
# Epic — Sleep, Consolidation & Continual Learning

## Why this matters (overarching goals: INNOVATIVE + VERSATILE)
The substrate already separates fast (W_fast) and slow (W_slow) timescales, but has NO offline consolidation — the brain's mechanism for turning transient experience into durable memory and for protecting old knowledge. Adding biologically-grounded SLEEP phases (memory replay + fast→slow consolidation + synaptic homeostatic downscaling, per the SHY hypothesis) is a genuinely novel continual-learning mechanism that this substrate is uniquely positioned to test. If it reduces catastrophic forgetting, it is a strong, differentiated contribution.

## What to build / Approach
A replay buffer that prioritizes high-surprise sequences; an offline consolidation phase that distills W_fast into W_slow (and gates by the bistable latch); synaptic homeostasis (global downscaling during "sleep" to prevent runaway potentiation); a wake/sleep scheduler woven into training and the chat loop; and a continual-learning benchmark (sequential tasks; measure forgetting bio-vs-vanilla).

## Acceptance criteria
- Sleep phases run without destabilizing training; consolidation measurably moves information W_fast→W_slow; continual-learning benchmark shows reduced forgetting vs vanilla (or a documented null).

Depends on: sax (working memory + bistable latch), vg9 (per-seq reset). Relates: 74f.
acceptance_criteria: Sleep phases run without destabilizing training; consolidation measurably moves info W_fast->W_slow (persists after fast reset); continual-learning benchmark shows reduced catastrophic forgetting vs vanilla (or a documented null).
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Design doc: sleep phases, replay & consolidation (SHY hypothesis)
description:
## Goal
Specify a biologically-grounded offline consolidation system: interleaved wake (online fast-weight learning) and sleep (replay + fast->slow consolidation + synaptic homeostatic downscaling per the Synaptic Homeostasis Hypothesis). Define what is replayed, how W_fast distills into W_slow, and how downscaling prevents runaway potentiation.

## Why
The substrate has fast/slow weights but NO mechanism to consolidate or protect memory offline — the brain's core trick for durable learning and avoiding catastrophic forgetting. This is a novel continual-learning angle this substrate uniquely enables.

## Acceptance
A committed design note with the wake/sleep cycle, replay policy, consolidation rule, and homeostasis rule, plus the eval plan.

Depends on: sax (online learning + bistable latch).

title: Replay buffer of high-surprise sequences + offline consolidation phase
description:
## Goal
Implement a prioritized replay buffer (rank sequences by surprise / loss / novelty) and an offline "sleep" phase that re-presents them to distill W_fast into W_slow, gated by the bistable consolidation latch (sax).

## Acceptance
Sleep phase measurably moves information from fast to slow weights (probe: post-sleep recall persists after fast-weight reset); runs without destabilizing training.

Depends on: cel.1.

title: Synaptic homeostatic downscaling during sleep
description:
## Goal
During sleep, globally downscale potentiated weights (multiplicative normalization) to restore dynamic range and prevent runaway potentiation from repeated wake-time Hebbian writes — the SHY mechanism. Preserve relative strengths (what was learned) while resetting absolute saturation.

## Acceptance
Downscaling keeps weight norms bounded across many wake/sleep cycles while preserving learned relative structure; tested.

title: Continual-learning benchmark (catastrophic forgetting, bio vs vanilla)
description:
## Goal
Build/borrow a sequential-task continual-learning benchmark (learn A -> B -> C, then re-test A) and measure forgetting for bio (with sleep/consolidation) vs vanilla and vs bio-without-sleep.

## Acceptance
Forgetting curves with stats (74f); a verdict on whether sleep/consolidation reduces catastrophic forgetting.

Depends on: cel.2, 74f stats.

title: Wake/sleep scheduler integrated into training and chat loop
description:
## Goal
A configurable scheduler that interleaves wake and sleep phases during training (e.g., every N steps) and optionally triggers a consolidation pass in the chat/inference loop between sessions. Logged in telemetry.

## Acceptance
Scheduler toggleable + logged; demonstrated in a training run and an inference session.

title: Roadmap v2 — Overview, Vision & Reading Order (START HERE)
description:
# Roadmap v2 — Overview, Vision & Reading Order  (START HERE)

This is the entry point for the "roadmap-v2" cohort (filter: `br list -l roadmap-v2`). It is fully self-contained: you do NOT need the root markdown plans to act on these beads. It was produced from a June-2026 ground-truth code audit + synthesis of the project's planning corpus.

## The one-paragraph thesis
The project's deepest problem and its biggest opportunity are the SAME thing: a large gap between the "living fluid" narrative and the actual computation. Today the biology is mostly DECORATIVE — the differentiable surface is a thin attention-logit bias plus a couple of EMAs, while online learning is inference-only, evolution is disconnected from its own credit signal, the headline kernels are dead code, and there is a silent optimizer bug. The ambitious plan: (1) make the biology REAL and TRUSTWORTHY, (2) make it genuinely LEARNABLE (differentiable dynamics) and SELF-EVOLVING (credit-assigned neurogenesis), (3) prove it with RIGOROUS science, then (4) push into frontiers no one else can reach because no one else has this substrate (sleep/consolidation, neuromodulated RL, in-silico neuroscience, calibrated uncertainty).

## Ground-truth findings the roadmap is built on (with evidence)
- TWO disjoint presynaptic impls: live `release()` (sigmoid/top-k) vs README's `forward()` (Hill/buffer ODE, `@no_grad`, dead — only neuroviz calls it). `synaptic.py:466`.
- Online Hebbian learning is INERT at train time — gated by `not torch.is_grad_enabled()` (`synaptic.py:861`).
- "Bistable CaMKII/PP1 latch" has no bistability; PP1 not in the gate (`synaptic.py:720`).
- "rank-R" eligibility is secretly rank-1 (mean broadcast, `synaptic.py:875`).
- NeuroScore "evolutionary credit assignment" is NEVER consumed by split/merge (0 hits in `synaptic_splitmerge.py`); expert count is constant (no real growth); "bank-account economy" doesn't exist.
- Optimizer-moment reset is a silent no-op (AdamW passed; weights are in Muon — `base_train.py:598` vs `gpt_synaptic.py:341`).
- Headline Triton presyn kernel (375 lines) is DEAD (zero callers); Rust kernel test-only + unbuilt; kernels target the dead equation; no Triton parity test.
- CMA-ES: only 10D; objective = train-loss on a toy copy task; real run was a documented NO-GO (signal 1.46e-4 vs noise 2.1e-3). The EVAL PROXY is the blocker, not the optimizer.
- NIAH stubbed (`eval_matrix.py:716`); no statistical testing anywhere; ~zero committed results.
- No logit softcap in synaptic model; fast weights/buffers leak across sequences; checkpoint reverts SynapticConfig to defaults (`checkpoint_manager.py:102`).

## The 14 epics (and the critical path)
FOUNDATIONS (do first, P0/P1):
- Truth & Integrity (8j9): unify presyn, honest docs, param census, golden tests.
- Numerical Robustness (vg9): softcap, hebbian-gate, optimizer-moment bug, per-seq reset, NaN guards, rank-R.
- Reproducible Platform (hm4): results registry+provenance, metrics schema, CI smoke, telemetry API.
CORE SCIENCE (P1):
- Differentiable Synaptic Dynamics (yw9) [FLAGSHIP]: make kinetics LEARNED, not hand-tuned — depends on Truth:unify.
- Online Learning & Working Memory (sax): real train-time Hebbian, true bistable latch — depends on Robustness fixes.
- Real Structural Evolution (uta): credit→lifecycle, function-preserving split/merge, variable count — depends on Robustness:optimizer-bug.
- Evaluation Rigor & Meta-Science (74f): fix the proxy (the blocker), NIAH, stats, redundancy/saturation, scaling laws.
- Hybrid Optimization (hea): SGD-learned + evolution-searched — depends on Eval:proxy + Diff:kinetics.
PERFORMANCE:
- Kernels & Backends (jyb): wire/delete dead kernels, single-pass fused LIVE kernel, diff backward, FlexAttention decode.
FRONTIERS / MOONSHOTS (P2):
- Sleep, Consolidation & Continual Learning (cel).
- Neuromodulation, Glia & Homeostasis (hy8): dopamine/ACh/NE three-factor learning + RL + the missing homeostatic safeguard.
- In-Silico Neuroscience: Interpretability (odq): probe/lesion/stimulate/connectome + flagship demo.
- Calibrated Uncertainty from Stochastic Release (u2t).
- Versatility & Reach (vap): synaptic retrofit, granularity knob, MGR geometry, model zoo.

## How to use this graph
- `br ready -l roadmap-v2` for unblocked work; start with Truth+Robustness (they unblock everything).
- Parent-child = epic→task→subtask; blocks = hard prerequisite (drives ready-work); related = cross-reference (incl. links to the legacy epics 114/gzm/0xd/6pj/4x9/acd).
- The single highest-leverage scientific bet is the FLAGSHIP epic (yw9, Differentiable Synaptic Dynamics): it converts ~48 brittle hand-tuned/CMA-ES-resistant kinetic constants into jointly-learned parameters and is the strongest potential paper.
- Overarching goals each epic serves are stated in its description (innovative / compelling / useful / accretive / powerful / robust / versatile).
notes:
ADDENDUM (v2 plan review): Added a 15th, cross-cutting foundation epic — "Testing, Logging & Validation Harness" (eqyk) — because the original cut under-specified how we PROVE features work. It provides: comprehensive unit tests, e2e test scripts for every major flow (training/inference/eval/CMA-ES/split-merge/online-learning/sleep/neuromod-RL/interpretability), a shared synthetic-task generator library, cross-backend parity + property/metamorphic/invariant tests, a performance-regression gate, detailed structured logging (rich + JSONL per-step bio-state), and a one-command VALIDATION REPORT that certifies "everything is working." Also added a feature-flag/ablation-toggle discipline task (hm4.7).

Every epic now carries a DEFINITION OF DONE in its Notes: a feature is not 'done' until it has unit tests + an e2e test in eqyk + detailed logging + a default-off toggle + docs, and tests pass AND logs demonstrate correct behavior. Build order tip: do eqyk.1 (test framework) + eqyk.2 (logging) + eqyk.3 (generators) EARLY (alongside the Truth/Robustness foundations) so every later feature lands fully tested and observable from day one.

title: Wire NeuroScore into the split/merge lifecycle (or reframe the claim)
description:
Claims-audit (8j9.1) gap: README presents NeuroScore (Efficiency/Specialization/Resilience, neuroscore.py:31) as the evolutionary credit-assignment engine driving split/merge. But synaptic_splitmerge.py has ZERO references to neuroscore — the lifecycle uses health=utilization*energy only (_health, L393). NeuroScore is computed but never consumed (appears only in neuroviz).

Decide + implement: either (a) feed NeuroScore metrics into the SplitMergeController's split/merge/reset selection (replacing or augmenting health), with tests showing the scores change lifecycle decisions; or (b) reframe the README to state NeuroScore is an observability metric, not a controller input. Coordinate with hm4.* (metrics schema).
close_reason: WIRED (option a): NeuroScore.step now publishes a per-expert composite fitness (Efficiency/Specialization/Resilience, min-max normalized then averaged, [0,1]) onto each SynapticMoE as last_neuroscore; SplitMergeController._health blends it into the health that drives ALL split/merge/reset selection. Behind SplitMergeConfig.use_neuroscore (default-off) + neuroscore_weight; base_train exposes sm_use_neuroscore/sm_neuroscore_weight. DDP-safe (rank0 decides+broadcasts; NeuroScore is rank0-only). 8 tests incl. proof that enabling it inverts split/reset/weak-slot selection. Full suite 237 passed. README reframe of this claim -> 8j9.3.

title: Epic — Testing, Logging & Validation Harness (proof everything works)
description:
# Epic — Testing, Logging & Validation Harness

## Why this matters (overarching goal: ROBUST + verifiable; nothing ships unproven)
This is a CROSS-CUTTING foundation epic. Every other epic's "Definition of Done" depends on it. The audit found the project has almost no tests for its live mechanisms and ~zero detailed run logging, so today there is no way to be SURE anything works after a change. This epic builds the machinery to PROVE — via comprehensive unit tests, end-to-end (e2e) test scripts, and rich, detailed logging — that every implemented feature behaves correctly. It exists so we can trust green = working.

## Scope (do NOT conflate with the science evals)
This epic is ENGINEERING CORRECTNESS, not scientific measurement. It overlaps-but-differs from the Evaluation epic (74f, which measures "does bio beat vanilla"): here we assert the code RUNS end-to-end, preserves invariants, is deterministic/reproducible, logs its state in detail, and does not regress. The two are cross-linked via `related` edges (e.g. an e2e test reuses an eval's synthetic generators and asserts the pipeline runs, while the eval task interprets the numbers).

## What to build
- A unit-test framework + conventions + fixtures + coverage gate.
- A structured, detailed LOGGING infrastructure (rich console + JSONL event logs) capturing per-step bio-state (calcium/RRP/energy/CaMKII/PP1/BDNF/lineage), metrics, seeds, and config — so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
- A shared synthetic-task generator library (copy, associative recall, NIAH, variable-binding, continual tasks) reused by tests AND eval suites.
- E2E test SCRIPTS for every major flow: training, inference/decode, eval matrix, CMA-ES, split/merge lifecycle, online-learning/working-memory, sleep/consolidation, neuromodulated RL, in-silico-neuroscience probing.
- Cross-backend parity, property-based/metamorphic/invariant tests, a performance-regression harness, a validation-report generator, and full CI integration (fast unit per-PR; nightly full e2e/perf with log-artifact upload).

## Acceptance criteria
- Every live mechanism has unit + e2e coverage with assertions; coverage gate enforced in CI; runs emit detailed structured logs; a validation report can certify "everything is working" with evidence; perf regressions fail CI.

## Definition of Done standard (this epic DEFINES it for all other epics)
See each epic's Notes: every feature ships unit tests + e2e coverage here + detailed logging + a default-off toggle + docs, and is not 'done' until tests pass AND logs demonstrate correct behavior.

Relates: hm4 (provenance/metrics/CI smoke), Epic gzm/4x9 (eval/viz). Foundational for ALL epics.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Unit-test framework, conventions, fixtures & coverage gate
description:
## Goal
The backbone every other test builds on. Establish pytest structure + conventions for this codebase: shared fixtures (tiny GPTSynaptic factory, seeded RNG, CPU/GPU skip markers, temp run-dirs), markers (`unit`/`e2e`/`slow`/`gpu`), deterministic-seed helpers, golden-artifact loaders, and a coverage config with an enforced minimum gate on the bio modules (synaptic.py, synaptic_splitmerge.py, neuroscore.py, kernels/, engine.py).

## Why
Tests today are ad-hoc (only BDNF + Rust parity). A consistent framework makes every later test fast to write and trustworthy. Comprehensive coverage of the LIVE mechanisms is the point.

## Acceptance
Fixtures + markers + seed helpers committed; coverage gate runs locally and in CI; a sample unit test for each bio module exists; documented in a TESTING.md.

Blocks the e2e/parity/property/perf/validation tasks in this epic.
close_reason: Completed: unit-test framework + fixtures + coverage gate + TESTING.md; full suite green, gate passing, lint/type clean

comments.author: TurquoiseFinch
comments.text: Delivered (TurquoiseFinch): tests/_bio_testkit.py (set_seed, tensor_stats/summarize/assert_finite, tiny model factories, golden I/O, capability probes), tests/conftest.py (autouse determinism, seed/rng/device/cuda_device/tmp_run_dir + tiny-model fixtures, gpu auto-skip), tests/test_framework_smoke.py (15 framework self-tests + sample unit test per bio module), pyproject.toml (markers unit/e2e/slow/gpu/golden + --strict-markers + pythonpath=['.'] + branch coverage config with fail_under=25), TESTING.md. Also fixed tests/test_rustbpe.py to importorskip rustbpe (was breaking collection). Verified: full non-slow suite 69 passed / 4 skipped; coverage 34.4% > 25% gate; ruff clean; ty clean. Env note: install via 'uv sync --no-install-project --extra cpu' (maturin/rustbpe build quirk -> bead jyb.8).

title: E2E test script: wake/sleep consolidation + forgetting check
description:
## Goal
An e2e test running a wake->sleep->wake cycle on a 2-task sequence, asserting: consolidation moves information W_fast->W_slow (post-sleep recall survives a fast-weight reset), homeostatic downscaling keeps weight norms bounded across cycles, and forgetting of task A is reduced vs a no-sleep control. Logs consolidation + norm trajectories.

## Acceptance
e2e sleep-cycle test passes; consolidation + homeostasis logs produced.

Depends on: framework + logging + datagen. Relates: cel.2, cel.3, cel.4.

title: E2E test script: neuromodulated three-factor / RL micro-run
description:
## Goal
An e2e test asserting the neuromodulatory bus broadcasts DA/ACh/NE, the three-factor rule consolidates ONLY reward-relevant associations on a toy reward task, and a short neuromodulated RL micro-run completes without divergence. Logs neuromodulator levels + eligibility consolidation per step.

## Acceptance
e2e neuromod/RL test passes; neuromodulator + reward-gated-plasticity logs produced.

Depends on: framework + logging. Relates: hy8.1, hy8.2, hy8.3.

title: E2E test script: probe / lesion / stimulation API smoke
description:
## Goal
An e2e test exercising the in-silico-neuroscience APIs: probe returns well-formed bio-state traces, lesioning a head/expert measurably changes output, and clamping/injecting synaptic state produces the predicted behavioral shift. Verifies the toolkit works on a real tiny model and logs the causal experiment.

## Acceptance
e2e interpretability smoke passes; probe/lesion/stim logs produced.

Depends on: framework + logging. Relates: odq.1, odq.2, odq.3.

title: Cross-backend parity test harness (Triton/Rust/Python, golden-driven)
description:
## Goal
A parity harness that asserts Triton, Rust, and Python backends compute the UNIFIED presyn equation identically (within tolerance), driven by the golden artifacts (8j9.4). Fixes the audit gap: today there is NO Triton-vs-anything parity test and Rust parity targets the dead equation.

## Acceptance
Parity test covers all available backends on the unified equation; auto-skips unbuilt backends with a clear message (no silent pass); runs in CI when backends are present.

Depends on: framework. Relates: 8j9.4 (golden), jyb.6 (dispatch), jyb.8 (rust build).

title: Property-based / metamorphic / invariant test suite
description:
## Goal
Beyond example-based tests: property/metamorphic tests asserting invariants that must hold for ANY input — vesicle conservation (RRP+RES+in-flight bounded), determinism under fixed seed, per-sequence reset semantics (sequence order independence after reset), monotonic depletion under sustained drive, stochastic-mode expectation matching the deterministic path in the limit, and NaN-freedom under extreme inputs. Use hypothesis-style generators.

## Acceptance
Property suite committed + green; each invariant documented with its rationale; runs in CI.

Depends on: framework. Relates: vg9.8 (numerical tests), yw9.2.2 (conservation).

title: Performance-regression test harness (committed baselines + gates)
description:
## Goal
A harness that measures tok/s + peak memory for representative train/decode configs, COMMITS baseline numbers, and FAILS CI on a >X% regression. The audit found benchmarks print but never persist/assert. Complements the kernel CUDA-graph/guardrail task (jyb.7) by being the general regression gate.

## Acceptance
Committed baselines; a nightly/perf CI job asserts no regression beyond tolerance; results logged to the registry.

Depends on: framework + logging. Relates: jyb.7, j9i, r2d.

title: Validation-report generator ('is everything working perfectly?')
description:
## Goal
A one-command generator that runs the full unit + e2e + parity + property + perf suites, aggregates results + key logs + coverage + bio-state sanity checks, and emits a human-readable VALIDATION REPORT (markdown/HTML) certifying what is verified-working, what failed, and with what evidence. This is the artifact that answers the user's requirement: "be sure everything is working perfectly after implementation."

## Acceptance
`scripts/validate_all.py` produces a timestamped, provenance-stamped report summarizing pass/fail + evidence per subsystem; committed sample report; wired into CI nightly.

Depends on: the e2e tasks + perf + parity. Relates: hm4.1 (results registry).

title: CI integration: fast unit per-PR + nightly full e2e/perf with log artifacts
description:
## Goal
Wire the whole harness into CI: a FAST job on every PR (unit + tiny e2e smoke + coverage gate), and a NIGHTLY job (full e2e + parity + perf-regression + validation report), uploading rich/JSONL logs + the validation report as build artifacts. Extends the existing CI (lint/type/rust/pytest) and the flagship-harness smoke (hm4.3).

## Acceptance
PR job green-gates on unit+coverage; nightly produces + uploads the validation report and logs; documented in CONTRIBUTING/TESTING.

Depends on: framework. Relates: hm4.3 (flagship smoke), .github/workflows/ci.yml.

title: E2E + invariant tests for theory artifacts (certificates/monitors/integrators) with detailed logging
description:
## Goal
A dedicated e2e + property/invariant test harness for the Leapfrog-Theory artifacts (epic 0642), so each thrust's compiled math is PROVEN to run and to satisfy its invariant after implementation — with great, detailed logging.

## What it checks (per active thrust, behind its toggle)
- Metriplectic (A): energy conserved to machine precision + free energy non-increasing over a real run; degeneracy residuals ~0; fallback fires on injected violation.
- Singular-perturbation (F): hysteresis present; retention tight at delta*; slow-manifold reconstruction within bound; eps-gated fallback.
- Thermo (E): empirical fluctuation-theorem (Crooks) residual within tolerance; TUR bound non-vacuous.
- Ultrametric (B): overlap-matrix tree-ness above threshold; retrieval energy descends per level.
- Free/TDA (C): predicted vs measured child spectrum; persistence-diagram stability.
- Tropical (H): tropical prediction matches soft argmax as T->0; certified radius lower-bounds adversarial radius.
- Gauge (D): gauge-invariance guard (W_total unchanged under regauge); holonomy computed on a toy loop.
- Sheaf (G): H^1=0 on consistent inputs, nonzero on inconsistent; diffusion converges to a section.

## Logging
Every check emits a rich summary + JSONL evidence (eqyk.2), feeding the validation report (eqyk.16).

## Acceptance
Runnable theory-artifact e2e suite; each active thrust's invariant asserted with logged evidence; integrated into CI nightly + the validation report.

Relates to every thrust artifact task and the validation-report generator (eqyk.16).

title: E2E + invariant tests for Capability-Frontier features (deliberation/adaptive-compute/hallucination/AI-scientist) with detailed logging
description:
## Goal
Dedicated e2e harness for the magnificence capabilities (epic r00r): assert each runs end-to-end behind its toggle and satisfies its core property, with detailed logging.
## Checks
- r00r.1 deliberation: F non-increasing across ponder steps; halting + energy-sampler work; latency bounded.
- r00r.3 adaptive compute: ATP budget respected; mean compute lower on easy tokens; quality floor held.
- r00r.5 hallucination detector: obstruction high on inconsistent inputs, low on consistent; action fires.
- r00r.2 AI-scientist: one full cycle runs within budget, logs provenance, proposes a mechanism.
## Acceptance
Runnable capability e2e suite; each active capability's property asserted with logged evidence; CI nightly + validation report (eqyk.16).
notes: COVERAGE: in addition to the flagship checks (M1 deliberation, M2 AI-scientist, M3 adaptive compute, M5 hallucination), this suite provides at minimum a SMOKE e2e + the core invariant for EVERY capability in epic r00r — M6 dream-replay (consolidation moves info), M7 model-card (certificate values present + validity gates), M8 bio-adapter (injects + runs on an external checkpoint), M9 working-memory (write changes generation), M10 fMRI viz (landscape renders), M11 curriculum (coverage improves), M12 curiosity-RL (exploration metric), M13 event-driven (speedup at bounded quality), M14 impossibility bounds (empirics respect them) — each behind its toggle, with detailed logging feeding the validation report (eqyk.16).

title: Structured, detailed logging infrastructure (rich + JSONL events)
description:
## Goal
The "great, detailed logging" backbone. A logging layer (built on the repo's `rich`/`logging` convention, NOT raw prints) that emits: (a) human-readable rich console output, and (b) machine-readable JSONL EVENT logs per run, capturing per-step bio-state (calcium/RRP/energy/CaMKII/PP1/BDNF/fatigue/lineage), losses/metrics, seeds, config hash, git SHA, and structural-plasticity events. Run-scoped log files under the run dir; configurable verbosity; correlation/run IDs; helpers to log a tensor summary (mean/std/min/max/NaN-count) cheaply.

## Why
After implementing a stateful bio mechanism we must be able to SEE its internals evolving to confirm correctness. Detailed logs are how e2e tests and humans verify "it's actually working," and how failures get diagnosed. Builds on / feeds the bio_telemetry() API (hm4.6).

## Acceptance
Logging helpers committed; a training/inference run produces both rich output and a parseable JSONL event log of bio-state per step; logs are gated by verbosity and cheap by default; documented.

Relates: hm4.6 (bio_telemetry), hm4.1 (results registry).
close_reason: Completed: structured rich-console + JSONL run-logging infra (RunLogger, tensor_summary, provenance, bio-state logging); 11 tests green, suite green, lint/type clean

comments.author: TurquoiseFinch
comments.text: Delivered (TurquoiseFinch): bio_inspired_nanochat/run_logging.py — RunLogger (run-scoped events.jsonl + console mirror via common.py's logging, NOT replacing it), run_id correlation, gather_provenance (git SHA/torch/cuda/host/pid + caller config_hash/seed), tensor_summary (JSON-serializable, never raises on NaN/Inf/empty), _jsonable coercion (tensors->summaries, NaN/Inf->strings), log_metrics/log_tensor/log_bio_state(per-step named bio-state tensors), context manager logging run_start/run_end/run_error, read_events roundtrip. tests/test_run_logging.py: 11 unit tests. Verified: full non-slow suite 80 passed/4 skipped; coverage>gate; ruff clean; ty clean. This is the logging backbone every monitor/detailed-logging bead writes through.

title: E2E test: uncertainty / calibration (MC ensembling, ECE, selective prediction) + detailed logging
description:
## Goal
Dedicated e2e for the Calibrated-Uncertainty epic (u2t) and the thermodynamic-UQ thrust (0642.3): run MC ensembling via stochastic vesicle release at inference, compute ECE + a risk-coverage (selective prediction) curve + OOD AUROC on a synthetic set, and assert the uncertainty-aware decoding action fires. Verifies the pipeline runs and is calibrated-ish, with detailed logging of per-sample predictive variance + the fluctuation-theorem residual (when thermo-UQ is on).
## Why
The Testing epic lacked any uncertainty/calibration e2e — a genuine coverage gap for a flagship capability (well-calibrated uncertainty + hallucination/trust signals).
## Acceptance
Runnable e2e producing ECE + risk-coverage + AUROC on synthetic data with logged predictive distributions; integrated into CI nightly + the validation report (eqyk.16).
Relates: u2t (MC ensembling u2t.1, calibration u2t.2), Thrust E (0642.3), r00r.5 hallucination detector.

title: E2E test: synaptic retrofit + MGR attention variants smoke + detailed logging
description:
## Goal
Dedicated e2e for the Versatility & Reach epic (vap) and the bio-adapter capability (r00r.8): (a) RETROFIT smoke — inject bio dynamics into a tiny external/HF-style checkpoint and run a few finetune steps asserting the dynamics are active and the model still produces valid logits; (b) MGR attention-variant smoke — instantiate each toggled attention variant (reversible/simplicial/ultrametric) + the ordinal scheduler and assert forward/backward run without NaN at parity with the baseline shape. Detailed logging of injected-state + per-variant timing/finiteness.
## Why
The Testing epic lacked any retrofit/MGR e2e — a coverage gap for the reach/versatility surface (which is how the project reaches beyond nanochat).
## Acceptance
Runnable e2e: retrofit + each MGR variant run cleanly behind toggles with logged evidence; CI nightly + validation report (eqyk.16).
Relates: vap (retrofit vap.1, reversible vap.3, geometric vap.4, ordinal vap.5), r00r.8 bio-adapter.

title: E2E + invariant tests for Capability-Frontier-II compositions (self-correct/metacognition/search/serving) with detailed logging
description:
## Goal
Dedicated e2e harness for the wave-2 compositions (epic re4e): assert each runs end-to-end behind its toggle and satisfies its core property, with detailed logging.
## Checks (per active composition)
- re4e.1 self-correct: a planted inconsistency is detected -> deliberated -> repaired (or certified abstain) within the iteration cap.
- re4e.2 metacognition: competence is calibrated + honest on planted known/unknown spans.
- re4e.3 energy search: search reduces final energy vs greedy at equal budget.
- re4e.4 lifelong memory: a fact taught in 'session A' is recalled in 'session B' post-consolidation, with per-user isolation + working 'forget'.
- re4e.5 serving engine: per-request knobs respected + SLA met-or-honestly-refused under a load test.
- re4e.7 speculative decode: output distribution unchanged, accept-rate logged. re4e.10 abstention: answered-subset error <= alpha.
- re4e.6/re4e.8/re4e.9/re4e.11/re4e.12/re4e.13/re4e.14/re4e.15: at minimum a smoke e2e + the core invariant, each behind its toggle.
## Logging
Every check emits a rich summary + JSONL evidence (eqyk.2) feeding the validation report (eqyk.16).
## Acceptance
Runnable wave-2 e2e suite; each active composition's property asserted with logged evidence; CI nightly + validation report.

title: Shared synthetic-task generator library (tests + evals)
description:
## Goal
One reusable, deterministic library of synthetic task generators used by BOTH the test harness and the science evals: copy/echo, associative recall, variable binding ("X has property Y" + distractors), needle-in-a-haystack (length-parametrized), simple continual-learning task sequences, and a reward task for RL. Seeded + reproducible + small enough for CI.

## Why
Avoids each eval/test reinventing generators (today the CMA-ES proxy, the working-memory suite, NIAH, and continual benchmark each imply their own). A shared lib keeps them consistent and makes e2e tests cheap.

## Acceptance
Generator library committed with deterministic seeding + tests; consumed by the working-memory suite (sax.4), NIAH (74f.2), continual benchmark (cel.4), CMA-ES proxy (74f.1), and the e2e tests here.

Relates: sax.4, 74f.2, cel.4, 74f.1.
close_reason: Completed: shared synthetic-task generator library (copy/recall/binding/NIAH/continual/reward), deterministic + model-compatible; 17 tests green, suite green, lint/type clean

comments.author: TurquoiseFinch
comments.text: Delivered (TurquoiseFinch): bio_inspired_nanochat/synthetic_tasks.py — one deterministic, model-compatible generator library (next-token, ignore_index=-1 matching the model CE, top-of-vocab control tokens): copy_task, associative_recall, variable_binding (contiguous [var,val] pairs + distractors between pairs), needle_in_haystack (length+depth parametrized), continual_task_sequence (disjoint vocab bands per task), reward_task (copy-first rule + reward_fn for RL). SyntheticBatch dataclass (inputs/targets/meta with answer positions+gold), TASKS registry + make_task(name). tests/test_synthetic_tasks.py: 17 tests — determinism, token-range, target masking, ANSWER-RECOVERABILITY per task (gold is actually derivable from the input), NIAH depth tracking, disjoint continual bands, reward rule, dispatch errors, and tiny-model consumption (finite loss). Fixed a real design flaw found by the recoverability test: distractors no longer split var-value pairs. Verified: full suite 111 passed/4 skipped; coverage>gate; ruff+ty clean. Unblocks eqyk.4/6/9/10; consumed by sax.4/74f.2/cel.4/74f.1.

title: E2E test script: full bio training run (tiny) with assertions + detailed logs
description:
## Goal
A runnable e2e SCRIPT + test that trains a tiny GPTSynaptic (all bio mechanisms ON) for a handful of steps on synthetic data and ASSERTS: loss decreases, no NaN/Inf, bio buffers (calcium/RRP/energy/camkii/bdnf) stay in expected ranges and actually CHANGE, checkpoint saves+reloads with identical config (round-trip), and the JSONL event log contains per-step bio-state. Emits detailed logs for human inspection.

## Why
The single most important "is the whole thing wired correctly?" check. Catches integration breakage across synaptic/optimizer/checkpoint/logging.

## Acceptance
Script `scripts/e2e/train_bio.py` (or tests/e2e/) runs in CI-time budget; all assertions pass; produces a readable log + JSONL trace artifact.

Depends on: framework + logging + datagen. Relates: vg9.6 (ckpt round-trip), vg9.7 (divergence guards).

title: E2E test script: inference/decode KV-cache + presyn-state parity
description:
## Goal
An e2e test that generates with the Engine (KV-cache + presyn-state carry/fork) and asserts: prefill-then-fork vs contiguous decode produce equivalent synaptic-state evolution (the parity gap the audit flagged as untested), sampling is deterministic under fixed seed, and bio-state is logged per decode step.

## Why
The audit found NO committed synaptic decode-parity test; presyn state forking is best-effort. This locks decode correctness.

## Acceptance
e2e decode-parity test passes (bio path, not just vanilla); detailed per-step decode logs produced.

Depends on: framework + logging. Relates: jyb.4 (flex decode), vg9.4 (per-seq reset), engine.py.

title: E2E test script: eval_matrix bio-vs-vanilla on synthetic data
description:
## Goal
An e2e test running the eval_matrix harness end-to-end on synthetic data for >=2 presets x >=2 seeds, asserting it produces schema-valid RunRecords, computes the stats layer (paired test + CI), and writes logs/CSV/JSONL. Verifies the pipeline (not the scientific result).

## Acceptance
Runs in CI-time; emits a valid summary with stats + detailed logs; fails if the harness breaks.

Depends on: framework + logging + datagen. Relates: 74f.3 (stats), 74f.4 (recipe-faithful), hm4.3 (CI smoke).

title: E2E test script: CMA-ES tune loop (resume) with detailed logs
description:
## Goal
An e2e test running tune_bio_params for ~2 generations on the synthetic proxy, asserting: progress.jsonl/best_params.json written, checkpoint resume reproduces state, the stagnation policy fires correctly on a flat objective, and detailed per-generation logs (candidates, scores, sigma, budget) are emitted.

## Acceptance
2-gen run + resume passes assertions in CI-time; detailed CMA-ES logs produced.

Depends on: framework + logging. Relates: hea.1 (objective), 74f.1 (proxy).

title: E2E test script: structural lifecycle invariants + lineage logs
description:
## Goal
An e2e test that forces split/merge/reset events and asserts: parameter shapes/router stay coherent, optimizer momentum is actually reset for changed params (across BOTH AdamW and Muon — guards the vg9.3 bug), function-preserving events cause no loss spike (guards uta.3), DDP determinism holds if multi-rank, and lineage events are logged.

## Why
Lifecycle is the riskiest mutation in the system and was barely tested (2 unit tests). This is the safety net for the whole Structural Evolution epic.

## Acceptance
Forced-event e2e test passes all invariant assertions; lineage + optimizer-state logs produced.

Depends on: framework + logging. Relates: uta.3, uta.5, uta.7, vg9.3.

title: E2E test script: online-learning / working-memory traces
description:
## Goal
An e2e test on an associative-recall task that asserts the online fast-weight mechanism ACTUALLY adapts within a sequence (W_fast changes with grad enabled — guards the vg9.2 inert-gate bug), improves recall of bound content vs a no-fast-weight control, resets per sequence (guards vg9.4), and the bistable latch shows hysteresis. Logs fast-weight norms + latch state per step.

## Acceptance
e2e online-learning test passes; detailed plasticity/latch logs produced; demonstrates the working-memory claim in code.

Depends on: framework + logging + datagen. Relates: sax.1, sax.2, sax.4, vg9.2, vg9.4.

title: CMAES: Phase 2 subgroup searches (48D staged)
description:
Goal: parallel subgroup CMA-ES (Calcium, Vesicle, Post, Structural, Energy) using Phase1-fixed top10.
Subtasks:
- Define per-group bounds and decoding.
- Run per-group jobs; capture best per subgroup; log covariances.
- Summarize subgroup winners.
acceptance_criteria: - Five subgroup CMA-ES runs (Calcium, Vesicle, Post, Structural, Energy) launched with Phase1-fixed params.\n- Best per subgroup saved with bounds/decoding noted.\n- Covariance heatmaps and summary table produced.

title: Eval: MoE health metrics in eval_matrix
description: Goal: log MoE health metrics (dead_expert_frac, specialization/gini, routing freq histogram) during eval_matrix runs. Subtasks: compute dead_expert_frac from router stats over eval window; add specialization/gini from neuroscore or lightweight stats; include fields in summary CSV/JSONL; document interpretation.

title: Wire or remove the router contrastive-update mechanism
description: Claims-audit (8j9.1) gap: router_contrastive_lr / router_contrastive_push params exist (gpt_synaptic.py) and the README table claims 'router embeddings + contrastive update', but no contrastive update code is wired into the lifecycle. Either implement the contrastive router-embedding update (push dissimilar experts apart) with a test, or remove the dead params and reframe the claim.
close_reason: FIXED (wire it): the router update was wired but ineffective (diagonal-only co-occurrence zeroed the pull term; empirically did not reduce pairwise similarity). Replaced with an effective similarity-weighted repulsion in SynapticMoE.forward that provably spreads router embeddings apart to specialize experts (0.946->0.909), gated by router_contrastive_push/_lr (0 disables). 5 tests lock the effect + disable switches + norm/finiteness. CLAIMS_AUDIT updated. Full suite 242 passed. README 'contrastive'->'lateral inhibition' wording -> 8j9.3.

title: Xpoll: Select transfer candidates

title: CORE eval: eval_bundle download 403 -> configurable URL/path
description:
The CORE eval runner (scripts/base_eval.py) currently tries to download eval_bundle.zip from:

- https://karpathy-public.s3.us-west-2.amazonaws.com/eval_bundle.zip

As of 2025-12-18 this URL returns HTTP 403 Forbidden, which blocks all CORE evaluation (including investigation of the SQuAD mismatch).

Work:
- Make the eval bundle source configurable via .env (python-decouple pattern) and/or CLI flags.
- Support a user-supplied local path to either the eval_bundle.zip file or an already-extracted eval_bundle/ directory.
- Improve error handling so a 403 gives a clear next-step message (what to set / where to place files).

Non-goals:
- Do not hardcode secrets or overwrite .env.
acceptance_criteria:
- base_eval can run without the S3 URL by using either a CLI-provided eval bundle path or a decouple-configured URL.
- If download fails (403/404), the error message tells the user exactly how to fix it (env var name + expected path).
- Add/adjust a small test or smoke command in docs verifying the bundle discovery logic.
close_reason: Completed

- Clear RuntimeError on HTTP failures with exact next-step instructions.
- Added tests/test_engine.py bundle-dir validation smoke tests.

title: Epic — Hybrid Optimization: SGD-learned + Evolution-searched
description:
# Epic — Hybrid Optimization: SGD-learned + Evolution-searched

## Why this matters (overarching goal: POWERFUL tuning)
The planning docs claim the highest-confidence ROI (15-25% perplexity) is systematic hyperparameter optimization — but the real Phase-1 CMA-ES run was a NO-GO because the objective was noise-dominated. The right architecture is HYBRID: make what is differentiable LEARNED by SGD (yw9), and SEARCH only the genuinely discrete/non-differentiable design choices (stochastic mode, granularity, kernel toggles, Ξ where SGD is unstable) with a properly-designed evolutionary loop. This epic delivers tuning that actually moves the metric.

## Current reality (ground truth)
- Only 10D implemented (not 48); objective reuses ONE seed every generation (noise never shrinks); controller doubles as a worker (GPUs idle); 48D scaling self-rated "no-go".

## What to build / Approach
Strengthen the objective (multi-fidelity ASHA/Hyperband, multi-seed averaging, composite quality+efficiency) on top of the fixed eval proxy; async distributed population eval (controller≠worker, pop>#GPUs); a census-driven full-parameter search with NO dead dimensions; a hybrid learn-vs-search protocol; and Ξ-genome evolution as an SGD alternative.

## Acceptance criteria
- A search run produces a reproducible, statistically-significant improvement on a held-out metric; the hybrid protocol is documented and demonstrated.

Depends on: 74f (proxy fix), yw9 (what to learn vs search). Relates: existing Epic 0xd, tasks 0c3.
acceptance_criteria: A search run produces a reproducible, statistically-significant improvement on a held-out composite metric; the hybrid SGD-learn + evolution-search protocol is documented and demonstrated to beat either alone.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Strengthen CMA-ES objective: multi-fidelity + multi-seed + composite
description:
## Goal
On top of the fixed proxy (74f.1), build a robust objective: multi-fidelity scheduling (ASHA/Hyperband-style) instead of fixed proxy-generations, multi-seed averaging per candidate, and a COMPOSITE score (held-out quality + a throughput/efficiency/regularization term) since bio claims are about capability/efficiency, not train loss.

## Acceptance
Objective is multi-seed, multi-fidelity, composite, and documented; reproduces a known improvement on the strengthened proxy.

Depends on: 74f.1.

title: Async distributed population evaluation (controller != worker)
description:
## Goal
Today the CMA-ES controller doubles as a worker (evaluates a slice itself, blocking) and population can't exceed #GPUs. Move to an async work-queue / Ray-style dispatcher so the controller only orchestrates and population size is decoupled from device count.

## Acceptance
Controller dispatches candidates to a worker pool; pop > #GPUs supported; fault-tolerant (lost worker -> requeue). 

Relates: existing bead 0c3 (distributed population eval harness).

title: Scale search to the full live parameter space (census-driven)
description:
## Goal
Once the objective gives signal, expand from 10D to the full LIVE parameter set — using the param census (8j9) so NO dead dimensions are searched. Stage by subsystem (calcium/vesicle/post/structural) per the original two-phase plan, but only after readiness gates pass.

## Acceptance
A full-space (census-validated, no no-op dims) search run completes with a statistically-significant held-out improvement.

Depends on: hea.1, 8j9.6.

title: Hybrid learn-vs-search protocol (SGD for differentiable, evolution for discrete)
description:
## Goal
Define and demonstrate the principled division of labor: kinetics that are now differentiable (yw9) are LEARNED by SGD; genuinely discrete/non-differentiable choices (stochastic mode, granularity, kernel toggles, structural thresholds) are SEARCHED by evolution. This is the right architecture given that the CMA-ES proxy struggled on smooth params SGD can handle directly.

## Acceptance
A documented protocol + a demonstration run where SGD learns kinetics while evolution searches discrete choices, beating either alone.

Depends on: yw9 (differentiable kinetics), hea.1.

title: Evolve the Xi-genome via CMA-ES/NES as an SGD alternative
description:
## Goal
For per-expert kinetics where SGD through the genome decoder is unstable or where discrete diversity is desired, evolve Xi vectors with CMA-ES/NES (neuroevolution). Compare against the SGD-learned genome (yw9.4).

## Acceptance
Xi-evolution implemented; head-to-head vs SGD-learned Xi on the rigorous eval.

Relates: yw9.4.

title: Epic — Reproducible Research Platform (tracking, CI, provenance)
description:
# Epic — Reproducible Research Platform

## Why this matters (overarching goal: USEFUL, cumulative science)
The project has THREE flagship harnesses (base_train, eval_matrix, tune_bio_params) but — per the audit — essentially ZERO committed results: `.research/anomaly-index.json` is empty, `artifacts/` holds placeholder JSONs, there is no `runs/` dir, and the only "CMA-ES results" are numbers hand-pasted into a markdown plan. Without a tracked, provenance-stamped result corpus and CI that exercises the harnesses, findings can't accumulate and code silently rots. This epic makes the project a platform that remembers what it learned.

## What to build / Approach
A committed, schema'd results registry (git SHA + full config hash + seed stamped on every run), a unified metrics schema shared across train/eval/tune/neuroscore, experiment-tracking wiring, enforced seed/determinism policy, a model-provided bio-telemetry API (replacing brittle deep-introspection in `engine.yield_metrics`), and CI smoke jobs so the three harnesses can't break unnoticed.

## Acceptance criteria
- Every run emits a provenance-stamped, schema-valid record to a tracked store.
- One metrics schema is the single source of truth; CI runs a tiny train + 1-gen CMA-ES + synthetic eval_matrix on every PR.
- A `bio_telemetry()` model API replaces ad-hoc introspection.

Serves: reproducibility, velocity, and is a hard dependency for 74f, hea, odq.
acceptance_criteria: Every run emits a provenance-stamped (git SHA + config hash + seed) schema-valid record to a tracked store; one metrics schema shared by all harnesses; CI smoke-runs all 3 flagship harnesses per PR; bio_telemetry() API replaces deep introspection.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Committed results corpus + experiment registry with provenance
description:
## Goal
A tracked, schema'd results store so findings accumulate. Every run (train/eval/tune) emits a record stamped with git SHA, full config hash, seed, hardware, dataset shard ids, and metrics.

## Why
Audit found ~zero committed results: empty anomaly index, placeholder artifacts, no runs/ dir, CMA-ES "results" only hand-pasted in markdown. Without this, every claim is unverifiable and work can't compound.

## Approach
Define a `RunRecord` schema (shared with the metrics-schema task); write to a committed JSONL registry (or wandb table + committed export); add a `results` query/CLI to summarize.

## Acceptance
Running any harness produces a provenance-stamped, schema-valid record in the registry; a summary command lists past runs.
close_reason: Results registry + RunRecord + provenance + query CLI; base_train emits records; tests green

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: DONE (commit pending) by TurquoiseFinch. New results_registry.py: RunRecord (provenance via checkpoint_manager git SHA + config hash; metrics validated via hm4.2 schema) + append/read JSONL registry (runs/registry.jsonl) + best-by-direction + summarize + query CLI. base_train emits a record at run end (rank 0, non-fatal). 8 tests; full suite 211 passed. eval/tune follow the same pattern.

title: Unified metrics schema across train/eval/tune/neuroscore
description:
## Goal
One canonical metrics schema (names, units, semantics) used by base_train, eval_matrix, tune_bio_params, and neuroscore — eliminating today's ad-hoc, per-script metric definitions.

## Why
Cross-harness comparison and the results registry require consistent metric identities. Extends existing infra intent (related bead d8l "Metrics schema unification").

## Acceptance
A single schema module imported by all harnesses; validation rejects unknown/duplicate metrics; existing metrics migrated.

Relates: existing bead d8l.
close_reason: Unified metrics schema module + validation + canonical set; tests green

New metrics_schema.py: MetricSpec registry (~23 canonical metrics across train/eval/tune/neuroscore) + validate_metrics (rejects unknown/duplicate/non-finite). 11 tests. hm4.1 (results registry) is the primary consumer; harness integration is incremental via the registry.

title: CI smoke tests for the three flagship harnesses
description:
## Goal
A fast CI job that exercises base_train (tiny: depth=4, ~20 steps), tune_bio_params (1 generation), and eval_matrix (synthetic data, 1 preset) end-to-end, so these harnesses cannot silently rot.

## Why
Audit: CI runs lint/type/rust/pytest but NO train/eval/HPO smoke; the three flagship scripts are exercised only by incidental imports.

## Acceptance
CI green job runs all three on synthetic/tiny configs in minutes; fails on import/runtime breakage.
close_reason: Smoke tests for train/eval/tune harness cores, in-process on tiny configs; smoke marker added; suite green

comments.author: ubuntu
