In-process on tiny configs (seconds), no rustbpe needed. 'pytest -m smoke' runs the subset. Full suite 216 passed.

title: Enforce and test seed & determinism policy
description:
## Goal
A documented, enforced determinism policy (seed plumbing, CUDA/NCCL flags, dataloader state) with a test that two same-seed runs match to tolerance. Extends existing bead aiq.

## Why
Reproducible bio-vs-vanilla science and noise-controlled HPO both require determinism; stateful bio buffers add seed-sensitivity surface area.

## Acceptance
Determinism doc + helper; same-seed reproduction test passes; flags recorded in every RunRecord.

Relates: existing bead aiq.

title: Wire experiment tracking into eval_matrix and tuner outputs
description:
## Goal
Connect harness outputs to the tracked store (wandb tables and/or committed JSONL) so eval matrices and CMA-ES progress are queryable, not just printed.

## Why
Audit: eval_matrix writes per-run CSV but never aggregates; CMA-ES writes progress.jsonl to an untracked run dir. Tracking makes results first-class.

## Acceptance
eval_matrix + tuner runs appear in the tracked store with provenance; a notebook/CLI can pull and compare.

title: Model-provided bio_telemetry() API
description:
## Goal
A clean `model.bio_telemetry()` API returning per-layer/head/expert bio-state (calcium, RRP, energy, camkii, pp1, bdnf, fatigue, lineage) as structured tensors/dataclasses.

## Why
`engine.yield_metrics` currently reaches into internals via brittle `hasattr` chains (`block.mlp.experts[*].fc1.post.camkii`) — fragile to refactors. A first-class API is needed for monitoring AND is the foundation for the In-Silico Neuroscience epic (probing/lesion/stimulation).

## Acceptance
Stable telemetry API with a schema; engine + neuroviz consume it instead of deep introspection.

Blocks: odq. Relates: neuroviz.py, engine.py.

title: Feature-flag & ablation-toggle discipline + config validation
description:
## Goal
Make every bio mechanism a clean, documented, default-OFF SynapticConfig toggle, with a registry of all ablation presets and a config VALIDATOR that rejects/​warns on invalid or conflicting flag combinations (and on dead flags, per the Truth epic). This keeps ablations scientifically clean and protects users from silently-broken configs.

## Why
The whole research thesis depends on "modular, toggleable mechanisms for clean ablation" (README). Today some flags are dead (`enabled`, `native_presyn`...), defaults are inconsistent, and there is no validation — so an ablation can be silently wrong. New mechanisms (differentiable dynamics, online learning, neuromodulation, sleep, glia) MUST each land behind a documented toggle.

## Build
- A single source of truth listing every mechanism flag, default, and the ablation presets that flip it (extends eval_matrix presets).
- A `validate(config)` that errors on conflicts (e.g. flex+decode, or a mechanism enabled without its prerequisite) and warns on non-default-risky combos.
- A test asserting each preset toggles exactly the intended fields.

## Acceptance
Config validator implemented + tested; ablation registry complete; every new mechanism is default-off and documented; relates to risk/rollback guardrails (existing co6).
close_reason: Implemented bio_inspired_nanochat/ablation_registry.py: a declarative MECHANISMS registry (every bio toggle + default + off-value + prerequisites, drift-guarded vs SynapticConfig), canonical ABLATION_PRESETS (eval_matrix now delegates to apply_preset — single source of truth), and validate_config()/assert_valid_config() that errors on opt-in-mechanism-without-prerequisite (e.g. bistable_latch sans enable_hebbian) + out-of-range knobs and warns on prefill-only flex. Wired into base_train (early validation). 26 tests incl. 'each preset toggles exactly its intended fields' + 'every preset validates clean'. Full suite 274 passed.

title: Epic — Neuromodulation, Glia & Homeostatic Control
description:
# Epic — Neuromodulation, Glia & Homeostatic Control

## Why this matters (overarching goals: INNOVATIVE + COMPELLING)
Real brains gate plasticity and exploration with GLOBAL neuromodulators (dopamine ≈ reward-prediction-error, acetylcholine ≈ uncertainty/attention, norepinephrine ≈ arousal/novelty) and stabilize themselves with GLIAL homeostasis. The project has none of this — and the predictions doc explicitly flags that the missing homeostatic safeguards are why positive-feedback mechanisms (BDNF, metabolism) risk winner-take-all/mode-collapse. Adding a neuromodulatory bus turns Hebbian learning into biologically-correct THREE-FACTOR learning and connects directly to RL (a transformer with a dopamine system that modulates its own RLHF). This is a flagship, differentiated direction.

## What to build / Approach
A global neuromodulatory bus (DA/ACh/NE scalars) that gates plasticity rate, exploration (stochastic-release fraction), and gain; three-factor reward-modulated Hebbian learning (eligibility × dopamine); RL integration (neuromodulated GRPO/RLHF in `chat_rl.py`); and an astrocytic/glial homeostatic layer (slow spatial pooling of metabolic state + synaptic scaling) that supplies the missing stability safeguard for uta and sax.

## Acceptance criteria
- Neuromodulators measurably gate learning/exploration; three-factor rule implemented + tested; an RL run shows sample-efficiency or stability effect; glial homeostasis demonstrably prevents a mode-collapse case.

Depends on: sax (Hebbian), vg9. Relates: uta (stability), u2t (ACh↔uncertainty), chat_rl.py.
acceptance_criteria: Neuromodulators (DA/ACh/NE) measurably gate plasticity/exploration; three-factor reward-modulated rule implemented+tested; a neuromodulated RL run shows a sample-efficiency/stability effect; glial homeostasis prevents a reproducible mode-collapse case.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Global neuromodulatory bus (dopamine / acetylcholine / norepinephrine)
description:
## Goal
Add a small set of GLOBAL neuromodulator scalars that broadcast to all synapses and gate behavior: dopamine (DA ≈ reward-prediction-error -> plasticity gain), acetylcholine (ACh ≈ uncertainty/attention -> exploration/stochastic-release fraction + input gain), norepinephrine (NE ≈ arousal/novelty -> global gain / reset). Computed from model signals (loss surprise, entropy, reward).

## Why
Real plasticity is GATED by global state; the project has none, which the predictions doc flags as a key missing safeguard and a key opportunity (three-factor learning, RL).

## Acceptance
Neuromodulator signals computed + broadcast + gating real quantities; telemetry exposes them; ablation shows they change learning/exploration.

title: Three-factor (reward-modulated) Hebbian learning
description:
## Goal
Turn the two-factor Hebbian rule (pre × post) into the biologically-correct THREE-factor rule (pre × post × neuromodulator): eligibility traces are gated/consolidated by dopamine so only reward-relevant associations stick. This is the canonical bridge between Hebbian plasticity and reinforcement learning.

## Acceptance
Eligibility × dopamine consolidation implemented + tested; on a reward task, only rewarded associations consolidate.

Depends on: hy8.1, sax (eligibility/online learning).

title: Neuromodulated RL integration (GRPO/RLHF) and sample-efficiency study
description:
## Goal
Wire the three-factor rule + dopamine=RPE into the RL loop (`scripts/chat_rl.py`) and test the hypothesis: does a transformer with a dopamine system that modulates its own plasticity learn from reward more sample-efficiently / stably than vanilla RLHF/GRPO?

## Why
A genuinely novel, compelling direction — and the bio mechanisms (stochastic exploration, reward-gated consolidation) map naturally onto RL.

## Acceptance
Neuromodulated RL run vs baseline with stats; a verdict on sample-efficiency/stability.

Depends on: hy8.2. Relates: chat_rl.py.

title: Astrocytic/glial homeostatic layer (the missing stability safeguard)
description:
## Goal
Add a slow GLIAL layer (tripartite synapse): astrocytes pool metabolic/activity state over groups of synapses/experts and apply homeostatic regulation (synaptic scaling, gliotransmission) to keep the network in a healthy dynamic range — supplying the homeostatic safeguard the predictions doc says is MISSING and that positive-feedback mechanisms (BDNF, metabolism) need.

## Why
Directly mitigates winner-take-all / mode-collapse risks for uta and sax; a novel computational-glia angle.

## Acceptance
Glial homeostasis demonstrably prevents a reproducible mode-collapse case (e.g., routing collapse) without hurting healthy training.

Relates: uta homeostatic guards.

title: Acetylcholine-gated exploration coupled to stochastic release
description:
## Goal
Couple ACh (uncertainty signal) to the stochastic-release fraction and input gain, so the model explores MORE (more vesicle noise) when uncertain and commits when confident — a principled, state-dependent version of today's fixed `stochastic_train_frac`.

## Acceptance
ACh dynamically modulates stochastic fraction; shown to track uncertainty; ablation vs fixed fraction.

Depends on: hy8.1. Relates: u2t.

title: Vis: Probe fast/slow weights & memory
description:
Goal: analyze fast vs slow weight behavior and in-context memory.
Subtasks:

- Plot decay curves of fast weights; show recall success vs distance.
- Report integrated into dashboards.

title: ty false-positives on optimizer param-groups in gpt_synaptic.setup_optimizers
description:
## Pre-existing issue (surfaced while doing vg9.1)
ty reports invalid-argument-type at gpt_synaptic.py setup_optimizers for the
AdamWFactory(adam_groups, ...) and MuonFactory(matrix_params, ...) calls: it infers
the DistAdamW/DistMuon branch's param-group type even on the non-DDP
partial(torch.optim.AdamW)/Muon path. These are NARROW-suppressed with
`# ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]` (documented inline) so the file is ty-clean.

## Fix options (when touched)
- Tighten the type of `adam_groups`/`matrix_params` (list[dict[str, Any]]) and/or the
  DistAdamW/DistMuon __init__ param annotations (adamw.py:15, muon.py:111) so ty can
  verify the call, then remove the suppressions.
- Or annotate the factory variables with a precise Callable type.

## Acceptance
ty passes on gpt_synaptic.py with the two `# ty: ignore[invalid-argument-type]`
suppressions REMOVED.

title: Perf: 4090 profiling & benchmark harness
description:
Goal: establish reproducible perf harness for dual RTX 4090 (PCIe, no NVLink).
Subtasks:
- Torch benchmarks for train/infer (bio and non-bio) with bs/seq sweep.
- Capture GPU util, mem, SM occupancy via nsight/torch.profiler.
- Scripts to pin GPUs, set env (CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES, NCCL_P2P_LEVEL).
- Baseline numbers logged for future regressions.
acceptance_criteria: - Benchmark scripts run on single and dual 4090 with fixed seeds; capture tok/s, latency, GPU util.\n- Profiles saved (torch.profiler/nsight) and referenced in repo docs.\n- Env/launch instructions documented for reproducibility.

title: Epic — High-Performance Kernels & Unified Backends
description:
# Epic — High-Performance Kernels & Unified Backends

## Why this matters (overarching goals: POWERFUL + ROBUST performance)
The "three-backend, dual-4090, 90% utilization" story is largely aspirational: the headline 375-line Triton presyn kernel is DEAD CODE (zero callers), the Rust presyn kernel is test-only with a dead control flag and isn't even built, and the only wired kernels (genetics/metrics/structural) are minor. Worse, the dead kernels implement the DEAD `forward()` equation, not the live `release()` path — so parity tests validate code nobody runs. This epic makes the performance substrate real, fused, differentiable, and measured.

## Current reality (ground truth)
- `presyn_step` (`kernels/presyn_fused.py:301`) has zero callers; "3-pass" recompute; hardcoded block sizes; no autotune.
- `native_presyn`/`native_plasticity` read by nothing; `rustbpe` not built → all Rust parity tests inert.
- FlexAttention raises `NotImplementedError` on KV-cache (`synaptic.py:1108`) and uses a non-equivalent bias formula; benchmarks force fp16 to dodge a bf16 atomic-add bug.

## What to build / Approach
Decide wire-or-delete for the dead kernels; build a single-pass fused kernel for the LIVE path; a DIFFERENTIABLE backward (to serve yw9); FlexAttention parity + decode; autotune + bf16 atomic fix; one unified device-aware backend dispatcher (not inconsistent native_* flags); CUDA-graph decode; build/ship Rust in CI; and committed perf-regression guardrails.

## Acceptance criteria
- No dead "backend" advertised; live path has a faster fused kernel with a committed benchmark + CI guardrail; Triton/Rust/Python parity is tested on the REAL equation; FlexAttention works in decode.

Depends on: 8j9 (unified presyn), yw9 (diff backward). Relates: Epic 6pj, tasks zsi/r2d/j9i.
acceptance_criteria: No dead backend advertised; the LIVE path has a faster fused kernel with committed benchmark + CI regression guardrail; Triton/Rust/Python parity tested on the unified equation; FlexAttention numerically-equivalent and decode-capable.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Decide wire-or-delete for the dead presyn kernels
description:
## Goal
Resolve the dead-backend problem honestly. The 375-line Triton `presyn_step` (`kernels/presyn_fused.py:301`) has ZERO callers; the Rust `presyn_step_cpu` is test-only with a dead `native_presyn` flag and isn't built. Both implement the DEAD `forward()` equation, not the live `release()`. Decide per backend: wire it to the unified live path, or delete it and stop advertising it.

## Acceptance
No kernel is advertised that isn't wired; deleted code's claims removed from docs; remaining kernels target the unified equation.

Depends on: 8j9.2. Relates: 8j9.3.

title: Single-pass fused presyn kernel for the LIVE path
description:
## Goal
Build ONE fused kernel that accelerates the equation the model actually runs (unified `release()`), not the dead `forward()`. Eliminate the redundant passes (the old Triton kernel recomputes Q·K + sigmoids in a 3rd pass) and the 5x `torch.zeros_like` + multiple `scatter_add_` allocations in the live Python path (`synaptic.py:358`).

## Acceptance
Fused kernel matches the canonical goldens; committed benchmark shows a measured speedup on the live path; CI guardrail (jyb.7) protects it.

Depends on: 8j9.2, 8j9.4.

title: Differentiable kernel backward pass
description:
## Goal
Give the fused presyn kernel a differentiable backward so the differentiable-dynamics epic can train AT KERNEL SPEED (not just in slow PyTorch). The dead Triton kernel had no backward and was forward/inference only.

## Acceptance
Kernel backward matches autograd reference (gradcheck on a small instance); usable in yw9 training.

Depends on: jyb.2, yw9 design.

title: FlexAttention parity + KV-cache decode support
description:
## Goal
Make the FlexAttention path usable and equivalent. Today it raises `NotImplementedError` on any prefix KV cache (`synaptic.py:1108`) so it's training/prefill-only, AND its `score_mod` computes a DIFFERENT bias than the standard path — so toggling flex changes the model. Fix both: numerically-equivalent bias + paged/decode score_mod.

## Why
FlexAttention is the right O(N) answer for the dense bias and is needed for efficient long-context + decode.

## Acceptance
Flex and non-flex produce numerically-equivalent outputs (within tol); flex works during decode; benchmarked.

title: Triton autotune + bf16 atomic-add fix
description:
## Goal
All Triton kernels currently use HARDCODED block sizes with no `@triton.autotune`/num_warps/num_stages, and benchmarks force fp16 to dodge a bf16 atomic_add bug. Add autotuning over block/warps/stages and fix the bf16 atomic path (accumulate in fp32) so training can run bf16.

## Acceptance
Autotune configs present; bf16 atomic-add works (no fp16 workaround needed); measured speedup recorded.

title: Unified device-aware backend dispatcher
description:
## Goal
Replace the inconsistent `native_*` flags (genetics honored, presyn/plasticity dead) with ONE dispatcher that auto-selects Triton (CUDA) / Rust (CPU) / PyTorch by device + extension availability, with a single override. Same equation across backends (8j9.2), validated by shared goldens.

## Acceptance
One dispatch entrypoint; correct backend chosen automatically; falls back safely; parity tests pass on the real equation.

title: CUDA-graph decode + committed perf-regression guardrails
description:
## Goal
Capture the static decode step with CUDA graphs (the per-step Python expert loop + state copies are graph-hostile today) for steady-state inference throughput, AND commit a perf benchmark with a CI guardrail that fails on >X% tok/s regression. Today benchmarks PRINT but never persist/assert.

## Acceptance
CUDA-graph decode path with measured throughput win; committed baseline numbers; CI regression gate active.

Relates: existing beads r2d, j9i, zsi; Epic 6pj.

title: Build and ship the Rust extension in CI (and decode its role)
description:
## Goal
The `rustbpe` extension (which also hosts the bio CPU kernels) is NOT built in the working checkout, so all Rust parity tests are inert (`skipif rustbpe is None`). Ensure CI builds + tests it (maturin), and clarify whether the Rust bio kernels are kept (CPU fallback) or retired (per jyb.1).

## Acceptance
CI builds the wheel and runs Rust parity tests against the unified equation; decision on bio-kernel retention recorded.

Depends on: jyb.1.

title: Bio: Dual sync/async release channels (Doc2)
description:
Goal: split release into synchronous (Syt1) and asynchronous (Doc2) channels.
Subtasks:
- Parallel masked paths; Doc2 gain param; config toggle.
- Tests ensuring async tail present on low calcium; NaN/inf guards.
- Perf regression check.
acceptance_criteria: - Sync and async release terms computed separately with Doc2 gain.\n- Unit test shows async tail on low Ca; disabled path matches baseline.\n- No NaN/inf; perf within 10%.
notes: Raised to P2 to align with other bio toggles; consider sequencing after stochastic release and metaplasticity work.

title: Bio: Stochastic vesicle release kernel + toggle
description:
Goal: add binomial/Gumbel-Sigmoid stochastic release as a configurable path.
Subtasks:
- Add config flag (SynapticConfig) and wire through SynapticPresyn.
- Implement Triton/PyTorch fused sampler; fallback to torch CPU.
- STE/Gumbel-Sigmoid relaxation for training; deterministic path unchanged.
- Unit tests: release mean/var vs analytical; perf regression <10%.
- Docs: README bio section + inline docstring on stochastic path.
acceptance_criteria: - Config flag enables/disables stochastic release path.\n- Unit test covers mean/var vs analytical and deterministic parity when disabled.\n- Benchmark shows <10% slowdown vs deterministic baseline.
close_reason: Completed

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Starting work on stochastic vesicle release toggle + implementation + tests (per bv highest-impact actionable). Agent Mail MCP not available in this environment (no resources), so coordinating via bd comments.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
Perf microbench (CPU, SynapticPresyn.release B=4,H=12,Tk=512,Tq=128,K=32, iters=30):
- deterministic (stochastic_train_frac=0): ~4.04 ms/iter
- stochastic_train_frac=0.12 with stochastic_mode=normal_reparam: ~4.19 ms/iter (+3.5%)
- stochastic_mode=gumbel_sigmoid_ste: ~6.94 ms/iter (+84.9%)
Recommendation: keep default stochastic_mode=normal_reparam for <10% overhead; gumbel_sigmoid_ste available for discrete Binomial-style sampling when desired.

title: Epic — In-Silico Neuroscience: Interpretability & Model-as-Brain
description:
# Epic — In-Silico Neuroscience: Interpretability & Model-as-Brain

## Why this matters (overarching goals: COMPELLING + VERSATILE)
Because this model has NAMED biological state — calcium, vesicle pools, CaMKII, energy, lineage — it can be studied like a brain in ways a vanilla transformer cannot. We can run lesion studies, "patch-clamp" probing, "optogenetic" stimulation, and connectomics of the evolving expert graph. This is a genuinely novel interpretability angle ("the neuroscience of a living transformer") and the project's best public-facing, compelling artifact. It also makes the other epics debuggable.

## Current reality (ground truth)
- `engine.yield_metrics` already reaches into `block.mlp.experts[*].fc1.post.camkii` via brittle `hasattr` chains; `neuroviz.py` + `LineageBook` record split/merge lineage. The pieces exist but are ad-hoc and not exposed as a coherent toolkit.

## What to build / Approach
A clean bio-state telemetry + "patch-clamp" probing API (read per-head/expert/token calcium/vesicle/CaMKII), a lesion/ablation toolkit (knock out heads/experts/mechanisms at inference for causal tracing), "optogenetic" stimulation (clamp/inject synaptic state to test causal role), expert connectomics + phylogenetic lineage visualization, and a flagship interactive demo.

## Acceptance criteria
- A documented probing/lesion/stimulation API; a connectomics+lineage viz; an interactive demo that lets a user run a causal experiment on the living model.

Depends on: hm4 (bio_telemetry API). Relates: Epic 4x9 (viz), neuroviz.py, dashboard.py.
acceptance_criteria: A documented probe/lesion/stimulation API on live bio-state; a connectome + phylogenetic-lineage visualization; an interactive demo letting a user run a causal experiment on the living model.
notes:
DEFINITION OF DONE — applies to EVERY child task of this epic (standard defined by Epic E_TEST / eqyk):
1. UNIT TESTS for the new/changed behavior — happy path + edge cases + failure modes, deterministic & seeded.
2. E2E COVERAGE — the feature is exercised by a real end-to-end run with assertions in the Testing epic (the relevant eqyk.* e2e script), not just isolated units.
3. DETAILED STRUCTURED LOGGING — rich console + JSONL event log of the mechanism's per-step state/metrics via the logging infra (eqyk.2), so behavior is observable and post-hoc verifiable.
4. TOGGLEABLE — every new mechanism behind a documented SynapticConfig flag, DEFAULT-OFF, registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7) for clean experiments.
5. DOCS/RUNBOOK updated; README claims kept truthful (epic 8j9).
6. QUALITY GATE — ruff --fix, ty, UBS clean; scripts/quality_gate passes.
NOT done until tests pass AND logs demonstrate the mechanism behaving correctly. Do NOT drop scope/features to make tests pass.

title: Bio-state probing API ('patch-clamp' for the model)
description:
## Goal
A clean read API to observe per-head/expert/token synaptic state (calcium, RRP, energy, CaMKII, PP1, BDNF, fatigue) during any forward/generation — the in-silico analog of patch-clamp recording.

## Acceptance
Built on `bio_telemetry()` (hm4); returns structured, schema'd traces; a notebook visualizes a probe of a real generation.

Depends on: hm4.6.

title: Lesion & ablation toolkit (causal tracing)
description:
## Goal
A toolkit to knock out heads/experts/mechanisms (or specific synaptic state) at inference and measure the behavioral effect — causal tracing of what each biological component does. Far richer than vanilla because the components are named and stateful.

## Acceptance
Lesion API (disable component X) + a study quantifying behavioral impact of lesioning each mechanism on a task battery.

title: 'Optogenetic' stimulation (clamp/inject synaptic state)
description:
## Goal
The complement to lesioning: CLAMP or INJECT synaptic state (force high calcium / deplete RRP / pin CaMKII ON) mid-generation to test the causal role of a mechanism in behavior — the in-silico analog of optogenetics.

## Acceptance
Stimulation API + a demonstration that clamping a mechanism produces the predicted behavioral change (e.g., forcing depletion increases novelty-seeking).

title: Expert connectomics & phylogenetic lineage visualization
description:
## Goal
Visualize the EVOLVING expert graph: routing connectome (which tokens go where), specialization map, and a phylogenetic tree of split/merge lineage over training (the LineageBook already records events). Show capacity growing where data is complex.

## Acceptance
Interactive/exported viz of the connectome + lineage tree from a real run; integrated with neuroviz.

Relates: existing bead dow (NeuroViz v2), neuroviz.py LineageBook.

title: Flagship interactive demo: 'the neuroscience of a living transformer'
description:
## Goal
A compelling public-facing interactive demo where a user watches synaptic state evolve during generation AND runs causal experiments (lesion a head, stimulate calcium, trigger a sleep phase) and sees the behavior change live. This is the project's best story-telling artifact and recruiting/credibility tool.

## Acceptance
A deployable demo (extends dashboard.py / a web UI) wired to the probe/lesion/stim APIs on a real trained model.

Depends on: odq.1, odq.2, odq.3.

title: Unify Set-A/Set-B presyn parameter names into one canonical set
description:
S3 of 8j9.2. The live release() uses Set-A params (alpha_c, syt1_slope, syt7_slope, doc2_gain, nsf_recover, prime_rate, rec_rate, amp_load/leak, energy_fill/use) and forward() uses Set-B (alpha_ca, syt_fast_kd, syt_slow_kd, alpha_buf_on/off, alpha_prime/refill/unprime, energy_in/cost_rel/cost_pump, q_beta, qmax). Collapse to ONE consistent set on SynapticConfig.

CAUTION: Rust Config::from_py reads fields BY NAME; tune_bio_params + checkpoints reference these. Renames must update: rust_src config parsing, scripts/tune_bio_params, any saved-config loaders (coordinate with vg9.6 checkpoint round-trip). No back-compat shims (AGENTS.md) — migrate all readers in-place. Add a unit test asserting every canonical field is read by both the Python ref and the Rust Config path.

Acceptance: single param set; Rust + Python read identical names; tune script + tests updated; no orphaned/duplicate kinetic params.
close_reason: Dead Set-A presyn params removed (kernel- + checkpoint-safe); tune retargeted to canonical params; census tests green

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
URGENT context from ukxt migration (fresh-eyes): now that the STANDARD attention path runs release_canonical (Set-B faithful params), several Set-A params are DEAD for the default model and must be reconciled here:

DEAD for the canonical standard path (only affect legacy release(), used solely by the off-by-default flex path):
- alpha_c   -> canonical uses alpha_ca
- syt1_slope, syt7_slope -> canonical uses Hill syt_fast_kd / syt_slow_kd
- cpx_thresh -> canonical uses complexin_bias
- amp_load, amp_leak -> canonical freezes AMP (uses energy->qamp)

STILL USED by the canonical: tau_c, doc2_gain (Doc2 preserved), and the state-dynamics params nsf_recover, prime_rate, rec_rate, unprime_per_release, energy_fill, energy_use, plus all Set-B (alpha_ca, syt_fast/slow_kd, alpha_buf_on/off, alpha_prime/refill/unprime, energy_in/cost_rel/cost_pump, q_beta, qmax, tau_buf/prime/rrp/energy).

CONCRETE FALLOUT: scripts/tune_bio_params.py TOP10_PARAM_SPECS (L107-115) tunes tau_c, alpha_c, syt1_slope, syt7_slope, cpx_thresh — 4 of 5 are now DEAD for the default model, so CMA-ES would optimize parameters with no effect. or4t MUST update TOP10_PARAM_SPECS to the canonical's live params (e.g. tau_c, syt_fast_kd, syt_slow_kd, alpha_ca, complexin_bias, ...). This is now higher-value than before the migration.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text:
IMPORTANT dynamics finding (8j9.2 fresh-eyes-3): the canonical uses rho_c=exp(-1/tau_c) (faithful, matches forward()/README), but the LEGACY release() used tau_c as a RAW retention multiplier. With the default tau_c=0.85 this changes calcium memory drastically:
  legacy:    retention 0.850 -> half-life 4.26 steps (strong short-term plasticity)
  canonical: retention 0.308 -> half-life 0.59 steps (calcium barely persists beyond 1 step)
So the migration cut calcium short-term memory ~7x. Since synaptic SHORT-TERM PLASTICITY is the project's headline, the default tau_c=0.85 is now poorly matched to the canonical interpretation. The EQUATION is correct (faithful); the PARAMETER default needs re-tuning. To RESTORE the legacy calcium memory under the exp form, tau_c ≈ 6.15 (since exp(-1/6.15)=0.85). Action: or4t/tune_bio_params should re-tune tau_c (and the other kinetic time-constants tau_buf/tau_prime/tau_rrp/tau_energy, which forward() also treats as exp time-constants) for the canonical regime, and 4fw should validate that short-term plasticity strength is preserved. A 15-step random-data smoke-train cannot reveal this.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: DONE (commit pending) by TurquoiseFinch. Removed 6 dead Set-A params (alpha_c/syt1_slope/syt7_slope/cpx_thresh/amp_load/amp_leak) from SynapticConfig (82->76 fields). VERIFIED safe: rust+kernels read none of them (0 hits); checkpoint-safe via vg9.6 field filtering. tune_bio_params retargeted to the canonical live knobs (alpha_ca, syt_fast_kd, syt_slow_kd, doc2_gain). 3 census tests; full suite 192 passed. Completes the s3w9->qcj7->or4t cleanup chain.

title: Delete the legacy SynapticPresyn.release() body (superseded by release_canonical)
description:
After ukxt migrated the live attention path to release_canonical, the legacy sigmoid release() (synaptic.py ~L289-476, plus its _mix_prob helper L277-287) is off the forward path and marked deprecated. This is the final mechanical step of removing the release/forward divergence.

Scope:
- Replace release()'s body with a thin delegation to release_canonical, OR remove release() entirely and have any remaining caller use release_canonical.
- Remove _mix_prob if no longer referenced.
- Update tests/test_presyn_canonical.py::test_buffer_ode_becomes_active_unlike_release: the 'contrast vs release()' half assumes release() leaves BUF unchanged; once release() delegates to the canonical it will change BUF, so drop/adjust that contrast.
- NOTE: the method body has trailing-whitespace blank lines (W293) — match exactly or strip first. No codemod scripts (AGENTS.md); edit by hand.

Acceptance: no legacy sigmoid release body remains; full suite green; release()/release_canonical are a single implementation.
close_reason: Legacy release() + _mix_prob deleted; release_canonical is the single source of truth

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Correction (from 8j9.2 fresh-eyes): the body's 'off the forward path' is now inaccurate — release() is still on the experimental FlexAttention forward path (it advances presyn_state for flex). qcj7 is correctly blocked by s3w9 (flex migration); release() can only be deleted once flex stops calling it. Until then it is NOT fully off the forward path.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: DONE (commit pending) by TurquoiseFinch. Deleted the legacy sigmoid release() + _mix_prob (~206 lines) now that nothing calls them (flex migrated in s3w9). Single source of truth = release_canonical; forward() remains as the @no_grad reference. Stripped trailing WS first (prep commit) for a clean match. Updated 3 test files (BUF-contrast simplified; tau_c tests -> release_canonical; spy asserts release gone; removed _mix_prob test). Full suite 190 passed; ruff+ty clean.

title: CMAES: Phase1 go/no-go summary
description:
Goal: formalize go/no-go after Phase1.
Subtasks:
- Summarize Phase1 results vs thresholds; include cost, seed variance, early-stop triggers.
- Recommendation on Phase2 + scope adjustments.
- Commit summary doc and notify stakeholders.
acceptance_criteria: - Formal go/no-go summary after Phase1: results vs thresholds, costs, seed variance, early-stop outcomes.\n- Recommendation for Phase2 scope; doc shared/committed.

title: Eval: Perf contrast bio vs vanilla
description:
Goal: quantify train/infer throughput & memory bio vs vanilla.
Subtasks:
- Run perf harness on key configs (vanilla, bio-all, top ablation).
- Record tok/s, latency, peak mem on dual 4090.
- Summarize deltas and recommend defaults for perf-sensitive users.
acceptance_criteria: - Perf harness runs on vanilla, bio-all, and top ablation configs on dual 4090.\n- Tok/s, latency, peak memory logged; deltas summarized.\n- Recommendations for perf-sensitive users documented.

title: Epic — Capability Frontier: turn the theory into world-class, magnificent capabilities
description:
# Epic — Capability Frontier (Magnificence)

## Why this epic exists (overarching goal: turn machinery into MAGNIFICENCE)
The roadmap has built extraordinary scientific machinery (differentiable synaptic dynamics, structural evolution, the 8-thrust Leapfrog Theory with its certificates and monitors, a full eval/interpretability/repro apparatus). This epic CASHES THAT MACHINERY OUT into headline, demo-able, world-class CAPABILITIES that conventional transformers structurally cannot offer. Every item here is deliberately ACCRETIVE: it reuses an existing subsystem and turns it into a user-facing power.

## The capabilities (each builds on an existing subsystem)
- r00r.1 Free-energy DELIBERATION + energy-based decoding (a metabolic System-2) — Thrust A free energy (0642.1) + decode.
- r00r.2 The AI NEUROSCIENTIST: closed-loop self-improving research flywheel — 74f/hea/odq/hm4.
- r00r.3 METABOLIC ADAPTIVE COMPUTE (energy-proportional inference) — metabolism + structural + decode.
- r00r.4 MACHINE-CHECKED THEOREMS (Lean) — formally verify the Leapfrog guarantees.
- r00r.5 SHEAF HALLUCINATION/INCONSISTENCY DETECTOR — Thrust G H^1 obstruction (0642.8) as a trust signal.
- r00r.6 Generative REPLAY / dreaming (extends Sleep cel). r00r.7 CERTIFIED-SAFE model + live certificate MODEL CARD.
- r00r.8 Cross-architecture BIO-ADAPTER library (extends retrofit vap.1). r00r.9 Synaptic WORKING-MEMORY read/write API.
- r00r.10 fMRI free-energy/attractor VISUALIZATION (extends demo odq.5). r00r.11 Topology self-CURRICULUM. r00r.12 Intrinsic-motivation RL. r00r.13 Neuromorphic BACKEND. r00r.14 Information-theoretic IMPOSSIBILITY results.

## Definition of Done (applies to every child; standard from Epic eqyk)
Unit tests + an e2e test in the Testing epic (eqyk) + detailed structured logging (eqyk.2) + a default-off toggle (hm4.7) + docs. NOT done until tests pass AND logs/metrics demonstrate it working, with an honest eval vs a baseline (74f.3). Do not oversimplify; preserve all functionality.

## Acceptance criteria
- Each capability ships behind a toggle with tests + e2e + logging + an honest measured benefit (or documented null); the flagship demo (odq.5) showcases >=3 of them; a model card reports the live certificates.

Relates: Leapfrog Theory (0642), 74f (74f), odq (odq), cel (cel), vap (vap), eqyk (eqyk).
notes: DEFINITION OF DONE — every child capability: (1) unit tests for the behavior (happy + edge + failure); (2) an e2e test in the Testing epic (eqyk.19 / eqyk) exercising it end-to-end; (3) detailed structured logging (eqyk.2) of the capability's signals; (4) a default-off SynapticConfig toggle registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7); (5) an HONEST measured benefit vs a named baseline with stats (74f.3) — a null result is valid; (6) docs + quality gate clean. NOT done until tests pass AND logs/metrics demonstrate it working. Do not oversimplify; preserve all functionality.

title: Free-energy DELIBERATION + energy-based decoding (metabolic System-2)
description:
## Idea
Thrust A (metriplectic, 0642.1) gives the synaptic state a genuine free energy F it descends. Turn this into ADAPTIVE TEST-TIME COMPUTE: on hard tokens run extra free-energy-minimization iterations ("ponder") instead of one step; expose F as a confidence/effort signal and STOP early when F is low. Add ENERGY-BASED DECODING: sample from exp(-F/kT) for controllable/constrained generation.

## Why magnificent + accretive
o1-style "think longer" GROUNDED IN PHYSICS — the model relaxes toward a lower-energy (more self-consistent) state. No conventional transformer has a principled energy to descend. Cashes out Thrusts A + E into a headline capability; compute scales with difficulty.

## Considerations / risks
Must converge — use Thrust A's Lyapunov guarantee so extra iterations are SAFE/bounded; latency budget; fallback to single-step decode; toggle default-off.

## Acceptance
Deliberation improves a reasoning/consistency metric at controlled extra compute vs single-step baseline (74f.3); unit + e2e tests + detailed logging of F-trajectories.

Depends on: Thrust A (0642.1). Relates: jyb decode (jyb.4), u2t (u2t).

title: Subtask: design — free-energy deliberation loop, energy halting & Boltzmann decoder
description:
Specify: (a) the inference-time deliberation loop that runs extra metriplectic free-energy-minimization steps on the synaptic state per token; (b) the halting rule (stop when dF below epsilon or budget hit); (c) the energy-based/Boltzmann sampler p ~ exp(-F/kT). Use Thrust A's Lyapunov property so extra iterations are provably bounded/convergent. Define the compute-vs-quality control knob.
## Acceptance
Committed design note + the convergence argument + the API.

title: Subtask: implement deliberation loop + per-token F-halting + energy-guided sampler (toggle+fallback)
description:
Implement the deliberation loop, the free-energy halting controller, and the energy-based decoder, behind a default-off `deliberation` toggle; deterministic fallback to single-step decode. Bounded latency budget.
## Acceptance
Runs in the engine decode path; converges; reduces to baseline when off.

title: Subtask: unit + e2e tests + detailed F-trajectory logging
description:
Unit tests: F is non-increasing across deliberation steps (Lyapunov); halting fires correctly; energy-sampler matches the target distribution on a toy case; fallback works. E2E: a generation run logs the per-token F-trajectory + deliberation-step counts (eqyk.2). Detailed logging.
## Acceptance
Tests committed + green; F-trajectory artifacts produced.

title: Subtask: eval — does deliberation improve reasoning/consistency at controlled compute?
description:
Falsification: on a reasoning/self-consistency task, sweep deliberation steps and assert quality improves vs single-step at matched extra compute; report the compute-quality curve with stats (74f.3). Honest null allowed.
## Acceptance
Stats-backed compute-vs-quality curve + verdict.

title: fMRI for transformers: live free-energy & attractor-dynamics visualization
description:
## Idea
Real-time visualization of the synaptic free-energy LANDSCAPE + attractor/basin dynamics during generation (low-dim projection of the state descending F, with depletion-driven basin hopping) — the flagship 'see the brain think' demo.
## Why compelling
The most shareable artifact: a beautiful, intuitive window into a living transformer's cognition. Extends the demo (odq.5) + connectome viz (odq.4) with the energy geometry from Thrust A/B.
## Acceptance
Interactive viz of F-trajectory + basins on a real model; integrated into the flagship demo; unit tests for landscape computation + logging.
Depends on: Thrust A (0642.1), odq demo (odq.5). Relates: r00r.1.

title: Topology-driven self-CURRICULUM (fill persistent-homology holes)
description:
## Idea
Use Thrust C's persistent-homology coverage signal (0642.5) to drive a SELF-CURRICULUM: preferentially train on / synthesize data filling detected topological HOLES (poorly-covered regions).
## Acceptance
Curriculum improves coverage (smaller/fewer holes) + downstream loss vs uniform sampling at equal tokens; unit tests + logging.
Depends on: Thrust C (0642.5.1.2). Relates: 74f (74f).

title: Intrinsic-motivation RL via neuromodulated CURIOSITY
description:
## Idea
Use the neuromodulatory bus (hy8) for INTRINSIC MOTIVATION: curiosity reward = prediction-error / free-energy reduction (dopamine-as-RPE + novelty-as-NE), so the model explores driven by its own surprise during RL.
## Acceptance
Curiosity-driven RL improves exploration/sample-efficiency vs extrinsic-only on a sparse-reward task; unit tests + logging.
Depends on: hy8 RL (hy8.3), r00r.1 (free energy as surprise). Relates: 74f.

title: Neuromorphic / event-driven sparse execution backend
description:
## Idea
The stateful, sparse, depletion-gated recurrence is naturally EVENT-DRIVEN. Prototype an event-driven/sparse path (skip computation for depleted/low-calcium edges) for large speedups; assess neuromorphic mapping (Loihi/SpiNNaker) or sparse-GPU.
## Acceptance
Event-driven path skips provably-negligible computation with measured speedup at bounded quality loss; a neuromorphic-mapping feasibility note; unit tests + logging. (Exploratory.)
Depends on: jyb (jyb). Relates: r00r.3.

title: Information-theoretic IMPOSSIBILITY results (honest limits)
description:
## Idea
Use information theory + the TUR thermodynamic bound (Thrust E) to prove what the bio mechanisms CANNOT do: capacity/precision/energy impossibility results (rate-distortion bound on fast-weight memory; TUR floor on uncertainty precision per joule). Honest limits that SHARPEN the contribution and prevent over-claiming.
## Acceptance
>=2 impossibility/lower-bound results proven, with empirical curves shown to respect them; documented in the theory notes.
Depends on: Thrust E (0642.3). Relates: 74f (74f), 0642.

title: The AI NEUROSCIENTIST: closed-loop self-improving research flywheel
description:
## Idea
Close the loop on the experimental apparatus. An orchestrator that autonomously: (1) FORMS hypotheses about which bio-mechanisms help; (2) RUNS ablations + lesion/probe/stim studies (odq) and the eval matrix (74f); (3) INTERPRETS via the stats layer (74f.3) + redundancy analysis (74f.5); (4) UPDATES a knowledge base; (5) PROPOSES new mechanisms / searches (hea).

## Why magnificent + accretive
Superhuman research velocity, reusing EVERY piece of infra; itself a novel agentic-science contribution. The bio substrate's named, lesionable state makes autonomous causal discovery uniquely tractable.

## Considerations / risks
Strict compute BUDGET + safety guards (no destructive ops; only via existing harnesses); every auto-experiment stamped to the registry (hm4.1); human review of proposed mechanisms; pre-register hypotheses to avoid p-hacking.

## Acceptance
The flywheel runs a batch of ablation/lesion experiments, produces a stats-backed registry-logged report ranking mechanism contributions, and proposes >=1 testable new mechanism, under a budget cap; unit + e2e tests + full logs.

Depends on: 74f stats (74f.3), odq (odq.2), hm4 registry (hm4.1). Relates: hea (hea).

title: Subtask: hypothesis generator + pre-registration
description:
Build the component that proposes falsifiable hypotheses about bio-mechanism contributions (from the census, prior results, and interpretability signals) and PRE-REGISTERS them (metric, prediction, stopping rule) to the registry before running — preventing p-hacking.
## Acceptance
Generates pre-registered hypotheses with explicit metrics + predictions.

title: Subtask: experiment runner integration + budget & safety guards
description:
Wire the runner to the EXISTING harnesses only (eval matrix 74f, ablations, lesion/probe/stim odq.2) under a hard COMPUTE BUDGET and safety guards (no destructive ops). Every run stamped to the registry (hm4.1).
## Acceptance
Runs a batch of experiments within budget; all logged with provenance.

title: Subtask: result interpreter (stats + registry) + knowledge base
description:
Interpret results via the stats layer (74f.3) and redundancy/saturation analysis (74f.5); update a persistent KNOWLEDGE BASE ranking mechanism contributions + interactions; surface confirmed/refuted hypotheses.
## Acceptance
A queryable knowledge base updated from a real experiment batch.

title: Subtask: closed-loop orchestration + new-mechanism proposal + e2e + logging
description:
Close the loop: generate -> run -> interpret -> update -> PROPOSE a new mechanism/search (feeding hea), with human-in-the-loop review. E2E test of one full cycle on tiny configs; detailed logging of the whole trace.
## Acceptance
One full autonomous cycle completes + proposes >=1 testable mechanism; e2e green; full logs.

title: METABOLIC ADAPTIVE COMPUTE: energy-proportional inference (real ATP budget)
description:
## Idea
Make "energy-constrained efficiency" REAL. Today energy is a soft load-balancer. Add a hard per-sequence ATP BUDGET and allocate compute PROPORTIONAL TO DIFFICULTY: dynamic expert top-k, dynamic depth (early-exit when free energy F is low/confident), dynamic MC-sample count — all spending from the budget.

## Why magnificent + accretive
Turns the central metaphor into a USEFUL efficiency feature (green-AI / edge / cost): faster at equal quality. Reuses metabolism, structural plasticity, and r00r.1's free-energy halting.

## Considerations / risks
Quality floor (never sacrifice correctness below a threshold); measured tok/s-vs-quality curve; toggle default-off; fallback to fixed compute; connect to perf-regression (eqyk.15).

## Acceptance
At equal quality, lower mean compute/energy per token than fixed-compute baseline (Pareto improvement), budget respected; unit + e2e tests + detailed per-token compute/energy logging.

Depends on: structural/metabolism (uta), r00r.1 (free-energy halting). Relates: jyb (jyb.7), eqyk.15.

title: Subtask: per-token difficulty/energy router + ATP budget accounting
description:
Implement the hard per-sequence ATP budget + a per-token difficulty signal (from free energy F / entropy) and the accounting that debits compute actions from the budget.
## Acceptance
Budget respected exactly; difficulty signal correlates with token loss.

title: Subtask: dynamic depth early-exit + dynamic expert-k + dynamic MC-samples
description:
Implement the three adaptive levers spending from the budget: early-exit layers when F is low (confident), dynamic expert top-k, dynamic MC-sample count for uncertainty. Couples to r00r.1's free-energy halting.
## Acceptance
Levers implemented + gated by budget/confidence; reduce mean compute on easy tokens.

title: Subtask: quality-floor guard + fallback + unit/e2e tests + logging
description:
Quality-floor guard (never drop below a correctness threshold); fallback to fixed compute if violated; unit + e2e tests; detailed per-token compute/energy logging (eqyk.2).
## Acceptance
Tests green; quality floor enforced; per-token compute logs produced.

title: Subtask: efficiency eval — Pareto curve vs fixed-compute baseline
description:
Falsification: at equal quality, measure mean compute/energy per token vs fixed-compute baseline; produce the quality-vs-compute Pareto curve with stats (74f.3) + perf-regression integration (eqyk.15).
## Acceptance
Stats-backed Pareto improvement (or documented null).

title: MACHINE-CHECKED THEOREMS: Lean formalization of the Leapfrog guarantees
description:
## Idea
Formally verify the core Leapfrog guarantees in Lean: metriplectic Lyapunov stability (0642.1.1.5), the cusp retention bound delta* (0642.2.1.4), the TUR calibration relation (0642.3.1). Use a Lean<->Python proof-feedback loop to keep formal statements in sync with the implementation and surface bugs.

## Why magnificent + accretive
Elevates "provably-grounded" to MACHINE-VERIFIED: "first sequence model with formally verified stability/memory guarantees." Formalization routinely exposes hidden assumptions/bugs in math AND code; hardens the alien-artifact proof obligations.

## Considerations / risks
Scope tightly (2-3 theorems first); the formal model must faithfully reflect the discrete code (document the abstraction gap honestly); research-grade effort.

## Acceptance
>=2 core theorems machine-checked in Lean with the abstraction-to-code gap documented; proof-feedback loop in CI as a slow gate; bugs filed + fixed.

Depends on: theory notes (0642.1.1.5, 0642.2.1.4). Relates: eqyk (eqyk).

title: Subtask: formalize metriplectic Lyapunov stability in Lean
description:
Formalize and machine-check the metriplectic stability theorem (degeneracy => dE/dt=0, dS/dt>=0 => free energy is Lyapunov => bounded) in Lean, against an abstract model of the discrete integrator. Document the abstraction-to-code gap.
## Acceptance
Lean proof compiles; abstraction gap documented.

title: Subtask: formalize retention delta* + TUR calibration in Lean
description:
Formalize the cusp retention bound delta* (0642.2.1.4) and the TUR calibration inequality (0642.3.1) in Lean.
## Acceptance
Lean proofs compile for both; assumptions made explicit.

title: Subtask: Lean<->Python proof-feedback loop + CI gate + bug filing
description:
Wire a Lean<->Python proof-feedback loop that checks the formal statements stay in sync with the implementation; integrate as a slow CI gate; file + fix any bug the formalization surfaces.
## Acceptance
Feedback loop in CI; >=1 surfaced bug filed/fixed (or a clean bill documented).

title: SHEAF HALLUCINATION / INCONSISTENCY DETECTOR (certificate-backed trust signal)
description:
## Idea
Repurpose Thrust G's binding-consistency obstruction (H^1, 0642.8) as a practical HALLUCINATION / inconsistency DETECTOR: when token bindings fail to glue into a global section (H^1 != 0 / large sheaf-Laplacian residual), flag the output as unreliable + emit a certificate; optionally trigger consistency-diffusion repair or abstain/clarify.

## Why magnificent + accretive
A trustworthy-AI capability conventional transformers cannot offer: a built-in geometric self-consistency check WITH a certificate — not a separate trained verifier. Nearly free given Thrust G; composes with r00r.1 (deliberate more when inconsistent) + u2t.

## Considerations / risks
Calibrate the threshold against real labels (must not over-flag); evaluate on hallucination/faithfulness benchmarks; toggle default-off.

## Acceptance
Obstruction predicts hallucination/inconsistency above baseline (AUROC vs softmax-entropy / self-consistency) on labeled data, with calibrated thresholds; unit + e2e tests + detailed obstruction-norm logging.

Depends on: Thrust G (0642.8). Relates: u2t (u2t.2), r00r.1, 74f (74f.3).
design:
MVP / DE-RISK PATH (so this flagship is not blocked on the exploratory Thrust G, 0642.8):
Deliver in two stages. (1) MVP detector using a CHEAP self-consistency residual — a fixed
sheaf-Laplacian quadratic form delta^T·delta over the token graph (or a simple multi-sample
self-consistency / agreement score) — needing NO H^1 / operadic cohomology, shippable against
the current model now. (2) UPGRADE to the full certificate-backed H^1 obstruction once Thrust
G's consistency monitor (0642.8.2.1) lands. Threshold calibration (M5.1) and the abstain/repair
action (M5.2) are SHARED across both stages. This lets the capability deliver value early and
upgrade to the certified version later.

title: Subtask: obstruction-as-hallucination signal + threshold calibration
description:
Define the hallucination/inconsistency score from Thrust G's H^1 obstruction / sheaf-Laplacian residual; calibrate the decision threshold against labeled hallucination/faithfulness data so it does not over-flag.
## Acceptance
Calibrated score + threshold; reliability diagram.

title: Subtask: detector + abstain/repair action (toggle + fallback)
description:
Implement the runtime detector that emits a certificate + triggers an action (abstain / clarify / run consistency-diffusion repair / deliberate more via r00r.1), behind a default-off toggle; fallback = no-op flag only.
## Acceptance
Detector + action implemented; reduces to baseline when off.

title: Subtask: eval (AUROC vs baselines) + unit/e2e tests + logging
description:
Falsification: the obstruction predicts hallucination/inconsistency above softmax-entropy + self-consistency baselines (AUROC) on a labeled set; unit + e2e tests; detailed obstruction-norm logging (eqyk.2). Stats (74f.3).
## Acceptance
Stats-backed AUROC vs baselines; tests green; obstruction logs produced.

title: Generative REPLAY / dreaming for data efficiency & continual learning
description:
## Idea
Extend Sleep/consolidation (cel): during sleep the model GENERATES synthetic sequences (dreams) from its own slow weights and replays them to consolidate fast->slow WITHOUT storing real data.
## Why accretive/useful
Privacy-friendly continual learning; bio-faithful (hippocampal replay/dreaming). Reuses the sleep scheduler + bistable consolidation.
## Acceptance
Dream-replay improves continual-learning retention and/or sample efficiency vs no-dream on the benchmark (cel.4); unit + e2e tests + logging.
Depends on: cel (cel.2). Relates: 74f (74f.3).

title: CERTIFIED-SAFE model + live certificate MODEL CARD
description:
## Idea
Compose the per-thrust runtime certificates (stability 0642.1, retention delta* 0642.2, calibration 0642.3, robustness radius 0642.6) into a deployable GUARANTEE BUNDLE, and auto-generate a MODEL CARD reporting them as LIVE measured guarantees (with the assumptions each holds under).
## Why magnificent
Radical transparency + a differentiated product for safety-critical/edge. Reuses every thrust certificate; with r00r.4, machine-verified.
## Acceptance
Model-card generator emits live certificate values + assumptions + validity gates; a deployment check refuses certification if assumptions are violated; unit tests + logging.
Depends on: thrust certificates (0642.1/.2/.3/.6), hm4.1. Relates: r00r.4, hm4.

title: Cross-architecture BIO-ADAPTER library (inject bio into any HF transformer)
description:
## Idea
Generalize synaptic-retrofit (vap.1) into a reusable ADAPTER LIBRARY that injects the bio mechanisms (presyn dynamics, fast weights, metabolism, thrust toggles) into ANY HuggingFace transformer in a few lines + a light finetune recipe.
## Why accretive/reach
Makes the ideas applicable beyond nanochat; others adopt + cite. Turns the project into a platform.
## Acceptance
Library injects bio dynamics into a real external HF checkpoint; a short finetune shows dynamics active + model works; packaged + documented; unit + e2e tests.
Depends on: vap retrofit (vap.1). Relates: vap zoo (vap.6).

title: Synaptic WORKING-MEMORY read/write API (inspectable, editable scratchpad)
description:
## Idea
Expose the fast-weight/calcium working-memory state as a structured READ/WRITE API: an agent/user can inspect what the model is 'holding', inject facts directly into the scratchpad, or clear it.
## Why magnificent/useful
Novel UX impossible in stateless transformers: write a fact into working memory and have the model use it without re-prompting; audit memory for safety.
## Considerations
Safety (sandbox + validate writes); builds on bio_telemetry (hm4.6) + probe (odq.1) but adds WRITE.
## Acceptance
Read/write API with a schema; demo where injecting a fact changes generation as predicted; safety validation; unit + e2e tests + logging.
Depends on: hm4.6, odq.1, sax (sax.1).

title: Perf: CI perf guardrail benches
description:
Goal: automated perf regression guard for dual 4090.
Subtasks:
- Choose slim bench matrix (bio/non-bio, short/long seq, train/infer).
- Implement script to run benches and emit json/csv.
- Wire into CI (or manual pre-push hook) with thresholds; fail on >X% regression.
- Store baselines and update process documented.
acceptance_criteria: - Perf CI bench script outputs json/csv and covers agreed matrix.\n- Thresholds enforced (fail on >X% regression); documented update procedure.\n- CI/pre-push run produces green baseline on dual 4090.
notes: Future enhancement: integrate lint/type/UBS checks so CI fails if code health regresses even when perf passes.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Consider adding lint/type/UBS gate to CI alongside perf guardrails; currently not covered.

comments.author: ubuntu
comments.text: Note: tie CI perf guardrail with new lint/type/UBS gate (5yi) to catch correctness/regressions.

title: Epic — Capability Frontier II: Emergent Compositions & the Product Layer
description:
# Epic — Capability Frontier II (Compositions)

## Why this epic exists (overarching goal: emergent power from composition)
Wave 1 (epic r00r) delivered individual capabilities. The next level is not more isolated features but EMERGENT COMPOSITIONS — fusing wave-1 capabilities into product-grade powers that are MORE than the sum of their parts, and the SYSTEM/PRODUCT layer that makes the project adopted. Every item here explicitly COMPOSES or EXTENDS specific wave-1 capabilities (r00r.1..r00r.14) and/or theory thrusts; none duplicates them.

## The compositions (each names the wave-1 pieces it fuses)
- re4e.1 SELF-CORRECTING generation loop = r00r.5 detect -> r00r.1 deliberate -> regenerate -> recheck (reliable output).
- re4e.2 METACOGNITION / self-model = r00r.1 energy + r00r.5 obstruction + uncertainty -> honest "I know / I'm guessing / I don't know".
- re4e.3 ENERGY-GUIDED SEARCH & planning = free energy (r00r.1) as a value function for tree search / rollouts.
- re4e.4 PERSISTENT LIFELONG MEMORY = working-memory API (r00r.9) + sleep consolidation (r00r.6) across sessions.
- re4e.5 SYNAPTIC SERVING ENGINE + certified SLA = r00r.3 budget + r00r.5 trust-gate + r00r.7 card + r00r.1 deliberation as per-request knobs.
- re4e.6 Automated THEORY-DISCOVERY engine (r00r.2+r00r.4). re4e.7 Speculative decoding via the metabolic cheap-path (r00r.3).
- re4e.8 Energy-based CONSTRAINED generation (r00r.1). re4e.9 Faithful REASONING-TRACE decoder (r00r.1+r00r.9).
- re4e.10 Conformal CERTIFIED ABSTENTION (r00r.5+r00r.7+uncertainty). re4e.11 GENOME MARKETPLACE / behavior transplant (r00r.8+genome).
- re4e.12 Multi-agent NEURAL SOCIETY + shared synaptic memory (r00r.9+structural). re4e.13 Homeostatic SAFETY guardrails (glia/neuromod).
- re4e.14 Living-model DEBUGGER/IDE (r00r.10+interp). re4e.15 CURRICULUM-FROM-DREAMS (r00r.6+r00r.11).

## Definition of Done (standard from Epic eqyk)
Unit tests + an e2e test in the Testing epic (eqyk.22 / eqyk) + detailed structured logging (eqyk.2) + a default-off toggle (hm4.7) + docs. NOT done until tests pass AND logs/metrics demonstrate it working, with an honest measured benefit vs a baseline (74f.3). Do not oversimplify; preserve all functionality.

## Acceptance criteria
- Each composition ships behind a toggle with tests + e2e + logging + an honest measured benefit; the serving engine (re4e.5) exposes the wave-1 capabilities as a coherent API; the self-correction loop (re4e.1) measurably reduces hallucination/inconsistency end-to-end.

Relates: wave-1 (r00r), Leapfrog Theory (0642), eqyk (eqyk).
notes: DEFINITION OF DONE — every child composition: (1) unit tests (happy + edge + failure); (2) an e2e test in the Testing epic (eqyk.22 / eqyk) exercising it end-to-end; (3) detailed structured logging (eqyk.2) of the composition's signals + trace; (4) a default-off SynapticConfig/serving toggle registered in the ablation registry (hm4.7); (5) an HONEST measured benefit vs a named baseline with stats (74f.3) — null results valid; (6) docs + quality gate. Compositions must TERMINATE/stay bounded (use the underlying Lyapunov/budget guarantees) and FALL BACK to the simpler component on failure. NOT done until tests pass AND logs/metrics demonstrate it working. Do not oversimplify; preserve all functionality.

title: SELF-CORRECTING generation loop (compose M5 detect -> M1 deliberate -> regenerate -> recheck)
description:
## Idea (the killer composition)
Close the loop on reliability: when the hallucination/inconsistency detector (r00r.5, r00r.5) flags a span, trigger free-energy DELIBERATION (r00r.1, r00r.1) localized to that span, REGENERATE it, and RE-CHECK — iterate until the obstruction/energy drops below threshold or emit a certified ABSTAIN. The working-memory (r00r.9) can pin established facts so corrections stay consistent.

## Why magnificent + accretive
Turns three individual capabilities into RELIABLE OUTPUT — a model that catches and HEALS its own errors mid-generation, with a certificate. The single most useful thing the substrate uniquely enables; the centerpiece of a trustworthy product.

## Considerations / risks
Bounded iteration budget (no infinite loops — cap + fall back to abstain); ensure the loop terminates (use r00r.1's Lyapunov-bounded deliberation); avoid oscillation; toggle default-off.

## Acceptance (see subtasks)
End-to-end: measurably lower hallucination/inconsistency on a labeled set vs single-pass at bounded extra compute (74f.3); unit + e2e tests + detailed logging of the detect/deliberate/regenerate trace.

Depends on: r00r.5 (r00r.5), r00r.1 (r00r.1). Relates: r00r.9 (r00r.9), u2t (u2t).

title: Subtask: design — the detect/deliberate/regenerate/recheck control loop + termination guarantees
description: Specify the closed loop: trigger conditions (r00r.5 obstruction / energy threshold), span localization, the deliberation+regenerate step (r00r.1), the re-check, the iteration cap + certified-abstain exit. Prove termination (bounded iterations; energy monotone via r00r.1's Lyapunov property). Define the compute budget knob. ## Acceptance: design note + termination argument + API.

title: Subtask: implement the loop (toggle + budget cap + abstain fallback)
description: Implement in the engine decode path behind a default-off 'self_correct' toggle; hard iteration/compute cap; deterministic fallback to single-pass + abstain when the cap is hit or the loop fails to converge. ## Acceptance: runs end-to-end; bounded; reduces to baseline when off.

title: Subtask: unit + e2e tests + detailed loop-trace logging + eval vs single-pass
description: Unit: loop terminates within cap; abstain fires on non-convergence; a planted inconsistency is detected+repaired. E2E + eval: on a labeled hallucination/inconsistency set, assert lower error than single-pass at bounded extra compute (74f.3). Detailed logging of every detect/deliberate/regenerate iteration (eqyk.2). ## Acceptance: tests green; eval curve + verdict.

title: Conformal CERTIFIED ABSTENTION (selective prediction with a provable error bound)
description:
## Idea
Compose the hallucination detector (r00r.5), thermodynamic calibration (Thrust E / u2t), and metacognition (re4e.2) with CONFORMAL PREDICTION to deliver certified SELECTIVE PREDICTION: the model abstains so that the error rate on answered queries is provably below a target alpha (distribution-free, finite-sample).
## Why magnificent + accretive
A guarantee no conventional model offers: "answers with <= alpha error or abstains." Composes r00r.5/u2t/re4e.2; the rigorous core of re4e.5's trust-SLA.
## Acceptance
On a held-out set, the answered-subset error is <= target alpha with the conformal coverage guarantee; unit + e2e tests + logging of the abstention rate vs alpha.
Depends on: r00r.5 (r00r.5), u2t.2. Relates: re4e.2, re4e.5, r00r.7.

title: GENOME MARKETPLACE / behavior transplant (library of pre-evolved Xi profiles)
description:
## Idea
Extend the bio-adapter (r00r.8) + the learnable genome (yw9.4 / Xi): a curated LIBRARY of pre-evolved Xi genomes — each a behavioral profile / dynamics 'personality' (e.g., high-novelty, high-retention, low-energy) — that you transplant into a model to instantly retune its dynamics WITHOUT retraining weights.
## Why accretive/useful
Instant behavior switching + transfer; turns the genome into a reusable asset; composes r00r.8 + the genome decoder.
## Acceptance
>=3 distinct, characterized genomes that, when transplanted, produce the predicted behavioral shift on a probe battery; unit + e2e tests + logging.
Depends on: r00r.8 (r00r.8), yw9.4. Relates: hea Xi-evolution (hea.5).

title: Multi-agent NEURAL SOCIETY + shared synaptic-memory bus
description:
## Idea
Extend the working-memory API (r00r.9) + structural evolution: multiple bio-models with distinct genomes (re4e.11) sharing a fast-weight MEMORY BUS, specializing and coordinating — a society of synaptic minds that pool working memory and divide labor.
## Why accretive
Novel multi-agent substrate where agents share genuine (not just textual) memory; composes r00r.9 + re4e.11 + the evolutionary theme.
## Acceptance
A 2-3 agent society sharing memory solves a task one agent cannot (or faster), with isolation/safety on the shared bus; unit + e2e tests + logging.
Depends on: r00r.9 (r00r.9). Relates: re4e.11, uta.

title: Homeostatic SAFETY guardrails as a control-theoretic alignment layer
description:
## Idea
Extend glial homeostasis (hy8.4) + neuromodulation: use the homeostatic control loop to enforce SAFETY invariants — keep the model out of provably 'unsafe' dynamical regimes (runaway potentiation, attractor collapse, OOD energy states), with a barrier-certificate guard. A control-theoretic alignment layer grounded in the dynamics.
## Why magnificent + accretive
Safety as a forward-invariance GUARANTEE on the dynamics (not just RLHF), uniquely enabled by the stateful substrate; composes glia + the metriplectic stability (Thrust A).
## Acceptance
A barrier/homeostatic guard keeps the model in the safe set under stress tests (it cannot enter the unsafe regime); unit + e2e tests + logging of the safety margin.
Depends on: hy8 glia (hy8.4). Relates: Thrust A (0642.1), uta.6.

title: Living-model DEBUGGER / IDE (breakpoints on bio-state, step through cognition, edit state)
description:
## Idea
Extend the fMRI viz (r00r.10) + interpretability (odq): an IDE-like DEBUGGER for the living model — step through generation token-by-token, set BREAKPOINTS on bio-state conditions (e.g. 'break when obstruction > x' or 'when expert 7 fires'), inspect/EDIT synaptic state, and replay. The reasoning-trace (re4e.9) is the call stack.
## Why magnificent/useful
A developer superpower impossible for stateless models: debug the model's COGNITION like code. Composes r00r.10 + r00r.9 + re4e.9 + the probe/lesion/stim APIs.
## Acceptance
A debugger that hits a bio-state breakpoint, shows the state + reasoning trace, allows an edit, and resumes; unit + e2e tests + logging.
Depends on: r00r.10 (r00r.10), odq.1. Relates: re4e.9, r00r.9, odq.2/odq.3.

title: CURRICULUM-FROM-DREAMS (generative replay that fills topological holes)
description:
## Idea
Compose generative replay (r00r.6) + the topology curriculum (r00r.11): during 'sleep', the model DREAMS synthetic data targeted at the persistent-homology HOLES (Thrust C) in its representation manifold — self-generated curriculum that fills its own coverage gaps.
## Why accretive
A self-improving data loop: the model identifies what it doesn't cover (topology) and dreams exactly that (replay). Composes r00r.6 + r00r.11 + Thrust C.
## Acceptance
Dream-curriculum reduces topological holes + improves downstream loss vs undirected dreaming/uniform data at equal tokens; unit + e2e tests + logging.
Depends on: r00r.6 (r00r.6), r00r.11 (r00r.11). Relates: Thrust C (0642.5).

title: METACOGNITION / self-model layer (honest 'I know / I'm guessing / I don't know')
description:
## Idea
A metacognitive layer that reads the real bio-state — free energy (r00r.1), binding obstruction (r00r.5), thermodynamic calibration (Thrust E / u2t) — to maintain an explicit estimate of the model's OWN competence per span, and surface it as honest, grounded confidence ("known fact" vs "extrapolation" vs "I don't know").

## Why magnificent + accretive
Genuine metacognition from ACTUAL internal state, not a trained-in disclaimer — impossible for a stateless transformer to do faithfully. Powers trust, selective answering, and safer agents. Composes r00r.1 + r00r.5 + uncertainty; feeds re4e.1 (self-correct) and re4e.10 (abstention).

## Considerations / risks
Calibrate the competence estimate against ground truth (must be honest, not over/under-confident); avoid leaking spurious confidence; toggle default-off.

## Acceptance
A per-span competence signal that is CALIBRATED (reliability diagram) and predicts correctness above a softmax-confidence baseline (AUROC, 74f.3); unit + e2e tests + detailed logging.

Depends on: r00r.1 (r00r.1), r00r.5 (r00r.5). Relates: u2t.2, hm4.6 (telemetry), re4e.1.

title: Subtask: design — competence signal from {energy, obstruction, calibration} + honesty target
description: Define the per-span competence/confidence estimate as a calibrated function of free energy (r00r.1), sheaf obstruction (r00r.5), and thermodynamic calibration (Thrust E). Specify the honesty objective (calibrated, not over/under-confident) + the three-way report (known/guessing/unknown). ## Acceptance: design + the calibration target.

title: Subtask: implement the metacognition layer + report API (toggle)
description: Implement the layer (reads bio-telemetry hm4.6), emits per-span competence + the three-way label via an API, behind a default-off toggle. ## Acceptance: API returns calibrated competence on a real generation.

title: Subtask: unit + e2e tests + logging + calibration eval vs baseline
description: Unit: competence is monotone in the underlying signals; honest on planted known/unknown cases. Eval: reliability diagram + AUROC of competence-predicts-correctness vs softmax-confidence baseline (74f.3). Detailed logging of the competence trace (eqyk.2). ## Acceptance: tests green; calibration report.

title: ENERGY-GUIDED SEARCH & planning (free energy as a value function for tree search / rollouts)
description:
## Idea
Extend deliberation (r00r.1) from a single trajectory to SEARCH: use the synaptic free energy F as a value/heuristic for tree/beam search (branch on high-energy/uncertain tokens, prune high-energy branches) and for model-based ROLLOUTS in synaptic-state space (a learned world model). System-2 search with a physical value function.

## Why magnificent + accretive
A principled alternative to brute-force best-of-N: search guided by a real energy landscape, not just logprob. Reuses r00r.1's free energy; composes with re4e.1 (search toward consistency) and re4e.9 (the search tree IS a reasoning trace).

## Considerations / risks
Branching/compute budget (tie to r00r.3 adaptive compute); the energy must correlate with answer quality (validate first); toggle default-off; fallback to greedy/beam.

## Acceptance
On reasoning/search tasks, energy-guided search beats equal-compute logprob beam/best-of-N (74f.3); unit + e2e tests + detailed logging of the search tree + per-node energies.

Depends on: r00r.1 (r00r.1). Relates: r00r.3 (r00r.3), re4e.1, re4e.9.

title: Subtask: design — energy-as-value search (tree/beam branching, pruning, state-space rollouts)
description: Specify the search: free energy F as the value/heuristic; branch on high-energy (uncertain) tokens, prune high-energy branches, and roll out in synaptic-state space (a learned world model). Tie the branching/compute budget to r00r.3 (r00r.3). FIRST validate that F correlates with answer quality (gate the search on this). ## Acceptance: design note + the F-vs-quality validation plan + budget policy + API.

title: Subtask: implement energy-guided search / rollouts (toggle + fallback to beam)
description: Implement energy-guided tree/beam search + state-space rollouts behind a default-off 'energy_search' toggle; deterministic fallback to standard beam/greedy; hard compute/branch budget. ## Acceptance: runs end-to-end; bounded; reduces to beam when off.

title: Subtask: unit + e2e tests + search-tree logging + eval vs equal-compute beam/best-of-N
description: Unit: search reduces final energy + terminates within budget + matches beam when energy is flat. E2E + eval: on reasoning/search tasks, beats equal-compute logprob beam / best-of-N (74f.3). Detailed logging of the search tree + per-node energies (eqyk.2). ## Acceptance: tests green; eval verdict.

title: PERSISTENT LIFELONG MEMORY (working-memory API across sessions via sleep consolidation)
description:
## Idea
Promote the within-session working-memory API (r00r.9, r00r.9) to LIFELONG personal memory: persist + consolidate user-specific fast-weights across sessions via sleep (r00r.6, r00r.6), with consent/privacy controls, a memory-management UX (view/edit/forget), and namespacing per user.

## Why magnificent + accretive
A model that genuinely REMEMBERS YOU across time — a capability stateless transformers structurally lack. Composes r00r.9 + r00r.6 + the consolidation latch; a compelling, sticky product feature.

## Considerations / risks
Privacy/consent + a hard 'forget' (right-to-be-forgotten); prevent cross-user leakage (strict namespacing); bound memory growth (consolidation + pruning); safety review of injected memory; toggle default-off.

## Acceptance
Cross-session: a fact taught in session A is recalled in session B after a consolidation 'sleep', with per-user isolation + a working 'forget'; unit + e2e tests + detailed logging of memory state + consolidation events.

Depends on: r00r.9 (r00r.9), r00r.6 (r00r.6). Relates: sax.1, cel (cel).

title: Subtask: design — persistence schema, per-user namespacing, consolidation/forget, privacy threat model
description: Design: the persistent fast-weight memory schema; strict PER-USER namespacing (no cross-user leakage); the cross-session consolidation policy (via sleep r00r.6); the consent + hard FORGET (right-to-be-forgotten) semantics; a memory-growth bound (consolidation + pruning); and a privacy/safety threat model for injected memory. ## Acceptance: design note + threat model + the isolation/forget contract.

title: Subtask: implement cross-session persistence + sleep consolidation + forget API (toggle)
description: Implement: persist user-namespaced fast-weights across sessions; consolidate during sleep (r00r.6, r00r.6); a forget API that provably erases; bounded growth; behind a default-off 'lifelong_memory' toggle. ## Acceptance: persists + consolidates + forgets per the contract.

title: Subtask: unit + e2e tests + memory-state logging (isolation, forget, no-leak, recall)
description: Unit: per-user isolation (no cross-user leak), forget actually erases, growth stays bounded. E2E: a fact taught in 'session A' is recalled in 'session B' after consolidation. Detailed logging of memory state + consolidation/forget events (eqyk.2). ## Acceptance: tests green incl. isolation + forget; recall e2e passes.

title: SYNAPTIC SERVING ENGINE + certified SLA (wave-1 capabilities as per-request API knobs)
description:
## Idea (the product layer)
A production inference server that exposes the wave-1 powers as FIRST-CLASS, per-request knobs: deliberation depth (r00r.1), energy/compute budget (r00r.3), trust-gating (r00r.5/re4e.1 self-correct), and uncertainty/abstention (re4e.10) — with the certified model card (r00r.7) enforcing runtime SLAs (latency/quality/trust guarantees per request, refusing requests it cannot certify).

## Why magnificent + accretive
Turns the research capabilities into a DEPLOYABLE, differentiated product with guarantees no conventional serving stack offers (per-request latency/quality/trust tradeoffs + certificates). Composes r00r.1/r00r.3/r00r.5/r00r.7/re4e.1/re4e.10; the thing that makes the project adopted.

## Considerations / risks
Batching with heterogeneous per-request budgets (scheduler); graceful degradation under load; the SLA must be honestly measured + enforced; ops/observability (vital-signs dashboard); toggle/feature-flag per knob.

## Acceptance
A server exposing the knobs + SLA enforcement; a load test shows per-request budgets respected + SLAs met or requests honestly refused; unit + e2e tests + detailed request-level logging (knobs, certificates, outcomes).

Depends on: r00r.3 (r00r.3), r00r.5 (r00r.5), r00r.7 (r00r.7), r00r.1 (r00r.1). Relates: re4e.1, re4e.10, jyb (jyb.7).

title: Subtask: design — API surface (per-request knobs), SLA spec, scheduler, vital-signs ops
description: Design the serving API: per-request knobs (deliberation depth r00r.1, energy/compute budget r00r.3, trust-gate r00r.5/re4e.1, abstention re4e.10); the SLA spec (latency/quality/trust guarantees + the refuse-if-uncertifiable rule); the scheduler for heterogeneous per-request budgets; and a 'vital-signs' ops dashboard (bio-state health in production). ## Acceptance: API + SLA + scheduler + ops design committed.

title: Subtask: implement the server + knob handling + SLA enforcement
description: Implement the inference server exposing the knobs, wiring them to the wave-1 capabilities, and ENFORCING the SLA at runtime (honestly refuse / degrade requests it cannot certify). Behind feature flags per knob. ## Acceptance: server serves requests honoring the knobs; SLA enforced or request refused.

title: Subtask: implement heterogeneous-budget batch scheduler + graceful degradation under load
description: Implement a scheduler that batches requests with DIFFERENT per-request budgets/knobs efficiently, and degrades gracefully under load (shed deliberation depth before correctness). ## Acceptance: mixed-budget batches scheduled; degradation policy demonstrated under load.

title: Subtask: unit + load-test e2e + request-level logging (budgets respected, SLAs met/refused)
description: Unit: knob handling + SLA-refusal logic. E2E load test: per-request budgets respected + SLAs met OR honestly refused under concurrent mixed load. Detailed request-level logging (knobs, certificates, latency, outcome) + the vital-signs stream (eqyk.2). ## Acceptance: load-test e2e green; request logs produced.

title: Automated THEORY-DISCOVERY & conjecture engine (M2 proposes -> M4 Lean proves/refutes)
description:
## Idea
Extend the AI Neuroscientist (r00r.2, r00r.2): let it propose NEW mathematical thrusts/conjectures beyond the existing 8, formalize them, and route to the Lean proof-feedback loop (r00r.4, r00r.4) to PROVE or REFUTE — a self-extending theory program.
## Why accretive
Turns the research flywheel into a theory-GENERATING engine; composes r00r.2 + r00r.4. Genuinely novel (automated math discovery on a real substrate).
## Considerations
Strict human review of proposed theorems; honest refutation handling; budget. 
## Acceptance
The engine proposes >=1 formalizable conjecture, attempts Lean proof, and records the verdict; unit + e2e tests + logging.
Depends on: r00r.2 (r00r.2), r00r.4 (r00r.4).
